468	func TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst(t *testing.T) {
469		ctx := context.Background()
470		st := storetest.New(t)
471		mod := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "mod", "")
472		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
473	
474		// Create four articles in chronological order. Pin the second one
475		// (oldest pin candidate that isn't the very first row, so we can prove
476		// it bubbles to the top regardless of created_at).
477		titles := []string{"first", "second", "third", "fourth"}
478		ids := make(map[string]int64, len(titles))
479		for _, ti := range titles {
480			a, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, mod.ID, mod.UserID, ti, "")
481			if err != nil {
482				t.Fatalf("create %q: %v", ti, err)
483			}
484			ids[ti] = a.ID
485			time.Sleep(15 * time.Millisecond)
486		}
487		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, ids["second"], mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
488			t.Fatalf("pin: %v", err)
489		}
490	
491		got, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
492		if err != nil {
493			t.Fatalf("ListByBoard: %v", err)
494		}
495		if len(got) != 4 {
496			t.Fatalf("got %d articles, want 4", len(got))
497		}
498		// Expected order: pinned first ("second"), then unpinned by created_at DESC.
499		want := []string{"second", "fourth", "third", "first"}
500		for i, w := range want {
501			if got[i].Title != w {
502				t.Errorf("[%d] = %q, want %q (full order: %v)", i, got[i].Title, w, titlesOf(got))
503			}
504		}
505	
506		// Pin a second article — both should now be at the top, ordered by
507		// created_at DESC within the pinned group.
508		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, ids["fourth"], mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
509			t.Fatalf("pin fourth: %v", err)
510		}
511		got, _ = st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
512		wantMulti := []string{"fourth", "second", "third", "first"}
513		for i, w := range wantMulti {
514			if got[i].Title != w {
515				t.Errorf("multi-pin [%d] = %q, want %q (full order: %v)", i, got[i].Title, w, titlesOf(got))
516			}
517		}
518	}
519	
520	func TestArticles_Update_PreservesPinnedAt(t *testing.T) {
521		ctx := context.Background()
522		st := storetest.New(t)
523		mod := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "mod", "")
524		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
525		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, mod.ID, mod.UserID, "rules v1", "body")
526		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
527			t.Fatalf("pin: %v", err)
528		}
529		pinnedBefore, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
530		if !pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Valid {
531			t.Fatalf("pin precondition failed")
532		}
533		if err := st.Articles().Update(ctx, a.ID, mod.ID, store.RoleMod, "rules v2", "amended body"); err != nil {
534			t.Fatalf("Update: %v", err)
535		}
536		got, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
537		if !got.PinnedAt.Valid {
538			t.Errorf("PinnedAt cleared by Update — must be preserved")
539		}
540		if !got.PinnedAt.Time.Equal(pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Time) {
541			t.Errorf("PinnedAt mutated by Update: before=%v after=%v", pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Time, got.PinnedAt.Time)
542		}
543		if got.Title != "rules v2" {
544			t.Errorf("Update did not apply: title=%q", got.Title)
545		}
546	}
547	
548	func titlesOf(arts []*store.Article) []string {
549		out := make([]string, len(arts))
550		for i, a := range arts {
551			out[i] = a.Title
552		}
553		return out
554	}
555

```text
package tui

// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
type Screen int

const (
	ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
	ScreenBoardList
	ScreenBoardView
	ScreenArticleView
	ScreenPostCompose
	ScreenWBInbox
	ScreenWBCompose
	ScreenWBThread
	ScreenOnline
	ScreenMailInbox
	ScreenMailThread
	ScreenMailCompose
	ScreenPasswordChange
	ScreenAdminUsers
	ScreenArticleEdit
	ScreenArticleExport
	ScreenBoardSplash
	ScreenBoardBannerEdit
)

// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
type NavigateMsg struct {
	To           Screen
	BoardID      int64
	ArticleID    int64
	Recipient          string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
	MailID             int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
	MailThreadID       int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
	CounterpartyUserID int64  // for ScreenWBThread: which 1-to-1 thread to open
}

// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }

// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
type WBIncomingMsg struct {
	ID         int64
	FromUserID string
	Body       string
}

// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
// re-render.
type PushAddedMsg struct {
	ArticleID  int64
	UserUserID string
	Kind       string
	Body       string
}

// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
// counter from DB.
type MailIncomingMsg struct {
	ID         int64
	ThreadID   int64
	FromUserID string
	Subject    string
}

// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
	BoardID      int64
	ArticleID    int64
	AuthorUserID string
	Title        string
}

// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
	ArticleID int64
}

// ArticlePinChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod pins or unpins an article.
// Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article list so the
// pinned-first ordering and the [M] marker stay current across all live
// sessions viewing the same board. ArticleID + Pinned are advisory; the
// re-fetch is the source of truth.
type ArticlePinChangedMsg struct {
	BoardID   int64
	ArticleID int64
	Pinned    bool
}

// ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod changes an article's
// comments_mode (open / arrows_only / locked). Article-view models filter
```

```text
1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68
```

28			{label: "線上使用者 Online", hint: "see who's logged in", to: ScreenOnline},
29			{label: "信箱 Mail", hint: "persistent threaded mail", to: ScreenMailInbox},
30		}
31		// Admin gets an extra entry; positioned before the Quit row so the
32		// numeric shortcut for Quit always lands on the LAST item regardless
33		// of role (the "5 quits" shortcut becomes "6 quits" for admin).
34		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
35			items = append(items, menuItem{
36				label: "管理 Admin",
37				hint:  "manage user roles",
38				to:    ScreenAdminUsers,
39			})
40		}
41		items = append(items, menuItem{label: "離線 Quit", hint: "disconnect", to: -1})
42		return mainMenuModel{deps: deps, items: items}
43	}
44	
45	func (m mainMenuModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
46	
47	func (m mainMenuModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
48		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
49			switch k.String() {
50			case "q", "esc":
51				return m, tea.Quit
52			case "up", "k":
53				if m.cursor > 0 {
54					m.cursor--
55				}
56				return m, nil
57			case "down", "j":
58				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
59					m.cursor++
60				}
61				return m, nil
62			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
63				it := m.items[m.cursor]
64				if it.to == -1 {
65					return m, tea.Quit
66				}
67				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
68			case "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6":
69				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
70				if idx < len(m.items) {
71					it := m.items[idx]
72					if it.to == -1 {
73						return m, tea.Quit
74					}
75					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
76				}
77			}
78		}
79		return m, nil
80	}
81	
82	func (m mainMenuModel) View() string {
83		u := m.deps.User
84		nick := u.Nickname
85		if nick == "" {
86			nick = u.UserID
87		}
88	
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" SSH-BBS · %s (%s) ", nick, u.UserID)))
92		b.WriteString("\n\n")
93	
94		if u.LastLoginAt.Valid {
95			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("上次登入 %s · 累計登入 %d 次 · 發文 %d 篇",
96				u.LastLoginAt.Time.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
97				u.NumLogins,
98				u.NumPosts,
99			)))
100			b.WriteString("\n\n")
101		}
102	
103		b.WriteString("  ")
104		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu "))
105		b.WriteString("\n\n")
106	
107		for i, it := range m.items {
108			marker := "  "
109			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
110			if i == m.cursor {
111				marker = "▸ "
112				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-32s ", i+1, it.label))
113			}
114			b.WriteString(marker + row)
115			if it.hint != "" {
116				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
117			}
118			b.WriteString("\n")
119		}
120	
121		b.WriteString("\n  ")
122		b.WriteString(StyleHelp.Render(fmt.Sprintf("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-%d jump · ? help · q quit", len(m.items))))
123		b.WriteString("\n")
124		return b.String()
125	}
126
```

18	// errors, but multiple sessions can race against a mode change so the store
19	// is the source of truth). See ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.

21		ErrCommentsLocked     = errors.New("comments are locked on this article")
22		ErrCommentsArrowsOnly = errors.New("only arrow comments are allowed on this article")
23	)
24	
25	type PushKind string
26	
27	const (
28		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
29		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
30		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
31	)
32	
33	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
34		switch k {
35		case PushKindPush:
36			return "推"
37		case PushKindBoo:
38			return "噓"
39		case PushKindArrow:
40			return "→"
41		}
42		return "?"
43	}

45	type Push struct {
46		ID          int64
47		ArticleID   int64
48		UserID      int64
49		UserUserID  string
50		Kind        PushKind
51		Body        string
52		CreatedAt   time.Time
53	}
54	
55	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
56	
57	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
58	
59	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
60		p := &Push{}
61		var kind string
62		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
63			return nil, err
64		}
65		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
66		return p, nil
67	}
68	
69	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
70		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
71			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
72		)
73		if err != nil {
74			return nil, err
75		}
76		defer rows.Close()
77		var out []*Push
78		for rows.Next() {
79			p, err := scanPush(rows)
80			if err != nil {
81				return nil, err
82			}
83			out = append(out, p)
84		}
85		return out, rows.Err()
86	}
87	
88	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
89	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create

91	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
92		switch kind {
93		case PushKindPush:
94			return 1, nil
95		case PushKindBoo:
96			return -1, nil
97		case PushKindArrow:
98			return 0, nil
99		}
100		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
101	}
102	
103	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the

105	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
106	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
107	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
108		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)

19		height  int
20		scroll  int

22	
23		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
24		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
25		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
26		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
27		rendered      string

35	
36		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
37		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
38		pendingDelete bool
39		pushCursor    int
40	
41		// pickingCommentsMode: showing the 1/2/3 picker overlay for the
42		// mod-only M shortcut (open / arrows-only / locked).
43		pickingCommentsMode bool
44	}
45	
46	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
47	// to the current viewport.
48	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
49		if m.article == nil {
50			m.rendered = ""
51			m.renderedWidth = 0
52			return
53		}
54		width := m.width - 4
55		if width < 40 {
56			width = glamourFallbackWidth
57		}
58		m.rendered = renderMarkdown(m.article.Body, width)
59		m.renderedWidth = width
60	}
61	
62	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
63		ctx := context.Background()
64		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
65		if err != nil {
66			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
67		}
68		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
69	
70		ti := textinput.New()
71		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
72		ti.CharLimit = 80
73		ti.Width = 60
74	
75		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
76		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
77		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
78		m.renderBody()
79		return m
80	}

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-06 09:00:01Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./CLAUDE.md`

```text
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
8	`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
9	SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
10	is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
11	keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
12	post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
13	they remain usable as text-edit keys.
14	
15	For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
16	`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.
17	
18	## Common commands
19	
20	```bash
21	make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
22	make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
23	make watch              # auto-rebuild on .go/.sql changes; SIGINT-then-restart via `air`
24	make build              # produces ./sshbbs
25	make test               # plain go test
26	make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
27	make cover              # per-package coverage
28	make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*
29	
30	# focused testing
31	go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
32	go test -race ./internal/chat/...
33	
34	# manual two-user smoke
35	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
36	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
37	```
38	
39	If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
40	Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.
41	
42	## Architecture — the non-obvious parts
43	
44	### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
45	`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
46	`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
47	`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
48	handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
49	other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
50	stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
51	to load.
52	
53	This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
54	constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
55	not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.
56	
57	### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
58	We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
59	(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
60	to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
61	during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
62	the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
63	into it. Don't switch back.
64	
65	### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
66	`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
67	it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
68	a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
69	a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
70	broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.
71	
72	### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
73	For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
74	`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
75	connected session receives the message; each article-view model
76	ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
77	tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
78	filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
79	payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.
80	
81	Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
82	multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.
83	
84	### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
85	`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
86	inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
87	`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
88	transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
89	the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
90	the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
91	without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
92	(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
93	canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.
94	
95	### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
96	`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
97	migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
98	ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
99	them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
100	prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
101	`schema_migrations` table.
102	
103	### Data deliberately diverges from pttbbs in three places
104	1. **No recno-based article addressing** — `articles.id` (auto-increment)
105	   is opaque and never re-used. Bookmarks stay valid forever.
106	2. **bcrypt cost-12, not `crypt(3)` DES** — `auth.bcryptCost`.
107	3. **No binary shared memory / `userinfo_t` arrays** — presence is a
108	   `sync.RWMutex`-guarded `map[int64][]*Session` in `chat.Broker`.
109	
110	The full mapping (userec_t / boardheader_t / fileheader_t / `.DIR` /
111	推文 / 水球 / userinfo_t) is in `docs/ptt_trace_code/01..06_*.md`.
112	
113	## Testing layers (see `docs/testing.md` for the full doc)
114	
115	- **Layer 1**: plain `go test` for `auth` / `store` / `chat`. Most coverage lives here.
116	- **Layer 2**: direct `tea.Model.Update()` tests in `internal/tui/`. Use
117	  `package tui` (internal), not `tui_test`, to reach unexported screen
118	  constructors. Keybinding-parity tests are table-driven over
119	  `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` etc. — when adding new keys,
120	  extend the table.
121	- **No `teatest` dependency.** It's experimental (`x/exp`); we test
122	  `Update()` directly with `tea.KeyMsg{...}` synthetic input.
123	- **Always run `-race`.** Several tests (`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`,
124	  `TestBroker_Concurrency`) exist specifically as race-detector bait.
125	- **Any test needing a DB** uses `storetest.New(t)` which gives a
126	  fresh `t.TempDir()`-backed SQLite. Never share stores across tests.
127	
128	## Conventions worth preserving
129	
130	- **UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.** Use `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers
131	  (`PadRight`, `PadLeft`, `Truncate`, `Width`) for any list rendering
132	  that mixes ASCII and CJK — `lipgloss`'s automatic width is unreliable
133	  for double-width glyphs.
134	- **Form screens skip h/l navigation** so the keys remain available for
135	  text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`,
136	  `screen_wb` (compose half).
137	- **NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens.** Sub-models
138	  emit it from `Update`; `Root.navigate` is the single switch statement
139	  that constructs the new sub. When you add a new screen, add the case
140	  there and a `Screen*` constant in `messages.go`.
141	- **Window-resize forwarding**: `Root.Update` always forwards
142	  `tea.WindowSizeMsg` to the active sub before its own logic. When
143	  switching to a new sub, `Root.navigate` resends the last known size
144	  so the freshly-mounted screen lays out correctly on first render.
145	
146	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
147	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
148	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
149	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
150	
151	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
152	
153	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
154	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
155	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
156	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
157	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
158	
159	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
160	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
161	drift is caught immediately.
162	
163	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
164	
165	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
166	
167	   ```
168	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
169	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
170	   ```
171	
172	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
173	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
174	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
175	
176	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
177	
178	   ```
179	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
180	   ```
181	
182	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
183	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
184	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
185	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
186	
187	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
188	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
189	
190	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
191	
192	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
193	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
194	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
195	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
196	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
197	
198	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
199	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
200	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
201	
202	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
203	
204	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
205	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
206	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
207	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
208	   revisit adjacent decisions).
209	
210	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
211	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
212	
213	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
214	
215	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
216	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
217	capturing:
218	
219	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
220	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
221	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
222	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
223	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
224	
225	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
226	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
227	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
228	when-to-add rules.
229	
230	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
231	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
232	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
233	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
234	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
235	invariant.
236	
237	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
238	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.

### 4. Full-Text Search & Indexing

**Current state:**
- SQLite driver: `modernc.org/sqlite v1.50.0` (pure-Go, line from go.mod)
- **No FTS5 extensions detected:** grep found no `fts5`, `FTS5`, or `// build` tags anywhere in the codebase
- **No fulltext index:** Title has **no index at all** — a LIKE '%pattern%' search would be a full table scan on `articles`

**For LIKE '%pattern%' search:**
- An **unanchored LIKE cannot use a standard B-tree index** efficiently (SQLite requires left-anchored or indexed-by-collation for LIKE to use index)
- Would require either:
  - **FTS5 virtual table** (requires extension compile; modernc.org/sqlite can support it but would need explicit build tag / cgo config)
  - **Index on title** (helps prefix search `LIKE 'pattern%'`, not substring)
  - **Standard full-table scan** (acceptable for small boards, but scales poorly)

## Investigation Summary: Help Key (`?`) Wiring in SSH BBS

The user's suspicion is **incorrect** — `?` help is actually **fully implemented and wired**. Here's what exists:

### 1. Question Mark Key Handlers (grep results)

The only active handler is in **`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/root.go:93`**:

```go
if msg.String() == "?" && helpAvailable(m.deps, m.activeScreen) {
    m.helpVisible = true
    return m, nil
}
```

This is a global interceptor in the Root model that toggles `helpVisible = true`, which triggers the help overlay to render on the next View() call. The one string literal `"?"` at `help.go:174` is the help footer entry documenting this to users: `{"?", "show this help (any key dismisses)"}`.

### 2. Single-letter h for help vs vim navigation

**No conflict.** CLAUDE.md clarifies: on **list screens** (boards, articles, online users, etc.), `h` is vim-left-navigation; on **form screens** (compose, register, password-change, board-banner edit), `h` is reserved for text input. Neither context uses `h` as help. Only `?` opens help, and it's intentionally not bound to `h` to avoid stealing vim navigation.

### 3. Help Screen File

Exists: **`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/help.go`** (254 lines). This is the sole help-rendering module. It defines:
- `screenHelp` map (lines 33–169): per-screen keymaps for Main Menu, Board List, Article View, Water Balloons, Online, Mail, Admin Users, etc.
- `helpGlobals` array (lines 173–177): cross-screen shortcuts including `?`, Ctrl+U, Ctrl+C.
- `renderHelp()` function (lines 215–245): builds the full-screen overlay.
- `helpAvailable()` function (lines 199–204): gate that returns false on form screens and during register/password-change flows.

### 4. Global Root Key Interceptor

**`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/root.go`** lines 81–101 is the complete global handler:

- Line 83–85: Ctrl+C always quits.
- Line 88–91: If help is visible, any key dismisses it (swallows the keypress).
- Line 93–96: `?` opens help overlay **if `helpAvailable()` returns true** (i.e., not a form screen, not during register).
- Line 98–100: Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).

The Root Update handles `tea.KeyMsg` before forwarding to the active sub-screen, so `?` is caught globally and never reaches individual screens.

### 5. Messages.go Constants

**No `ShowHelpMsg` or `ScreenHelp` constant** — help doesn't use the message system. The `helpVisible` boolean flag in Root toggles inline; no custom message type needed.

### 6. Footer/Hint Text on Screens

**Every list/menu screen advertises the help key in its footer.** Examples:

- **Main Menu** (`screen_main_menu.go:122`): `"↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-5 jump · ? help · q quit"`
- **Board List** (`screen_board_list.go:104`): `"↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"` (no `?` hint here)
- **Article View** (`screen_article_view.go:634`): Variable footer; build with modal-specific keys; help key **not** mentioned.

The **inconsistency**: Main Menu advertises `? help`, but Board List and Article View do **not** mention `?` in their inline footer hints. They only document screen-specific keys and rely on the Root help overlay to advertise the global `?` key.

### Conclusion

**The ? help is fully wired and works correctly.** What's missing is **consistent UI messaging**: form screens suppress `?` entirely (by design — `?` becomes a literal character in text input), but non-form list screens don't all advertise it in their footer hints. The user likely pressed `?` on a form screen (compose, register, or password-change) where help is intentionally disabled so typing works. Main Menu footer does say "? help", so if the user tried there and it didn't work, that would be a bug — but the code shows it should work.

# Plan: board/article search + article sort + help-key polish

## Decisions (recommended)

- **Search activation key:** `/` on both list screens — vim-idiomatic, currently unbound, doesn't collide with existing keys.
- **Boards filter:** **client-side**. Board count is small (3 seeded, expected <100 lifetime). No DB churn; no migration.
- **Articles filter:** **server-side** via a new options struct. Loading 100 then filtering client-side gives empty results when the substring matches an article #101+. LIKE `%q%` won't use an index, but `WHERE board_id = ?` keeps the per-board scan tiny.
- **Sort key:** `s` cycles `Newest ↔ Score`. Pinned still floats to top in both modes.
- **Sort field:** **`recommend_score DESC`** (option A). No new column, no migration. Trade-off documented below.
- **Search-mode UX:** dedicated `searchActive bool` + `bubbles/textinput`. While active, every printable key (including `h`, `j`, `k`, `l`, `[`, `]`, `g`, `G`, `M`, `p`, `b`, `B`, `s`) routes to the textinput — same rule as form screens. Only `enter` (confirm), `esc` (cancel + clear), and arrows / `ctrl+u` / `ctrl+c` remain controls.
- **Help-overlay availability:** `?` already works (root.go:93). Issue is discoverability. Add `? help` to the footer hint on board list, board view, article view, and add `/`, `s` to `screenHelp`.

## Order of work

### 1. Store layer (foundation; everything else depends on it)

**`internal/store/articles.go` — extend ListByBoard**

Introduce an options struct so the API scales beyond the current two needs (filter + sort) without a third overload later:

```
type ArticleSort int
const (
    SortNewestFirst ArticleSort = iota // existing default
    SortByScoreDesc
)

type ListArticlesOpts struct {
    Limit       int
    TitleSearch string      // empty = no filter; LIKE '%q%' COLLATE NOCASE
    Sort        ArticleSort
}

func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoardOpts(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, opts ListArticlesOpts) ([]*Article, error)
```

Keep `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` as a thin shim → `ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, ListArticlesOpts{Limit: limit})`. Existing call sites (boardViewModel.Init, ArticleAddedMsg / ArticlePinChangedMsg / ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg handlers in screen_board_view.go, NeighboursOf is unaffected) keep compiling.

SQL shape:
```sql
SELECT ... FROM articles
WHERE board_id = ?
  [AND title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' COLLATE NOCASE]    -- only when TitleSearch != ""
ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL),
         CASE WHEN ?=1 THEN recommend_score END DESC,   -- when Sort=SortByScoreDesc
         created_at DESC, id DESC
LIMIT ?;
```
- Escape `%`, `_`, `\` in TitleSearch before binding (use `strings.NewReplacer`). LIKE pattern is `'%' + escaped + '%'`.
- Pinned-first stays first in ORDER BY for both sorts (matches existing semantics from articles.go:78 and the `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst` test).
- `created_at DESC, id DESC` is the secondary tiebreaker even in score mode — keeps results deterministic when many articles have the same score (very common for fresh posts at score=0).

**`internal/store/articles_test.go` — extend (file already exists, see articles_test.go:50, 468)**

New tests:
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_TitleSearch` — substring match, case-insensitive, doesn't escape into other boards.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_TitleSearch_EscapesLikeWildcards` — title `"50%"` doesn't match `"5"`.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_SortByScore` — three articles with scores 5, 0, 10 surface in 10/5/0 order.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_SortByScore_PinnedStillFirst` — pinned 0-score article precedes unpinned 10-score article.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_TitleSearchAndSort_Combined` — both filters apply; pinned-first preserved.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoard_BackwardCompatible` — explicit assertion that the old shim returns identical rows for `Limit=N` as the previous behavior.

No changes needed in `internal/store/boards.go` (client-side board filter).

### 2. Board list — client-side filter + footer hint

**`internal/tui/screen_board_list.go`** (currently 108 lines, see screen_board_list.go:13)

Add to `boardListModel`:
```
search       textinput.Model
searchActive bool   // input focused & accepting keys
filter       string // confirmed filter (kept after Enter)
filtered     []*store.Board   // recomputed whenever filter or boards changes
```

State machine:
- Idle (filter empty) — full keymap as today.
- Searching (`/` pressed → `searchActive = true`, textinput.Focus()) — every key except `enter`/`esc`/`ctrl+u`/`ctrl+c`/arrows passes to `textinput.Update`. Each keystroke recomputes `filtered`.
- Confirmed (`enter`) — `searchActive = false`, `filter = strings.TrimSpace(search.Value())`, cursor reset to 0. Filter persists; arrows / `j`/`k`/`h`/`l` work normally over `filtered`.
- Cleared (`esc` while idle but filter non-empty) — clear filter, `filtered = boards`, restore cursor to 0.
- Cleared (`esc` while searching) — drop the in-flight query AND any prior filter; back to idle.

Filter function (CJK-aware substring — Go's `strings.Contains` is byte-safe for UTF-8 substrings; case folding via `strings.EqualFold` per-rune is overkill, simply lowercase ASCII portions via `strings.ToLower` on both sides since board names are typically ASCII; if a board ever has CJK in `Name`/`Title` the lowercase is a no-op and substring-match still works):

```
func boardMatches(b *store.Board, q string) bool {
    if q == "" { return true }
    lq := strings.ToLower(q)
    return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Name), lq) ||
           strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Title), lq) ||
           strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Description), lq)
}
```

View:
- When `searchActive`: render textinput on its own line above the list with prompt `搜尋 / : `. Result count subline `(N matches)`.
- When `filter != "" && !searchActive`: render dim header `[搜尋: foo · N results · / 修改 · esc 清除]` above the list.
- Footer hint always ends with `… · / search · ? help · Ctrl+C disconnect`.

Cursor invalidation:
- After every filter recompute: clamp `m.cursor` to `len(filtered)-1` and to 0 if empty. Don't try to anchor by board ID — the user is actively typing, so jumpiness from anchoring would be worse than reset.

**`internal/tui/screen_board_list_test.go`** (extend; current at screen_board_list_test.go:1)

New tests:
- `TestBoardList_SlashEntersSearchMode` — typing `/` then `Te` then `enter` filters to `Test`, cursor at 0.
- `TestBoardList_SearchPreventsHJKLNav` — while searchActive, `h` types into the input, does NOT navigate back. Same for `j`/`k`/`l`/`Q`.
- `TestBoardList_EscClearsFilter` — after a confirmed filter, esc reverts to full list.
- `TestBoardList_FilterCursorClamps` — cursor=2 then filter narrows to 1 result → cursor=0.
- `TestBoardList_FilterByDescription` — query matches `Description` substring.
- `TestBoardList_FilterCJK` — query containing 「閒」 matches `ChitChat` board (whose title is `閒聊`).

### 3. Board view — server-side filter + sort toggle + footer hint

**`internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`** (currently 335 lines, see screen_board_view.go:13)

Add to `boardViewModel`:
```
search       textinput.Model
searchActive bool
filter       string                  // confirmed title filter
sort         store.ArticleSort       // SortNewestFirst (default) or SortByScoreDesc
```

Refactor the four current `ListByBoard` calls (lines 29, 43, 57, 73) into one helper:
```
func (m *boardViewModel) reload(anchorID int64) {
    arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoardOpts(ctx, m.board.ID,
        store.ListArticlesOpts{Limit: 100, TitleSearch: m.filter, Sort: m.sort})
    if err == nil { m.articles = arts }
    if anchorID > 0 { m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID) }
    if m.cursor >= len(arts) { m.cursor = max(0, len(arts)-1) }
}
```

State machine for `/` mirrors board list. `s` cycles sort (only when not searchActive); reload after each toggle, anchor by current article ID so the highlight follows the post.

Key gating during searchActive: every alphanumeric key — including `s`, `M`, `p`, `b`, `B`, `g`, `G`, `j`, `k`, `h`, `l`, `[`, `]` — must NOT trigger their action. Only `enter` confirms, `esc` cancels, `up`/`down`/`PgUp`/`PgDn` still scroll (handy: glance at filtered results while typing).

View additions:
- Search input row when `searchActive` (above the article table).
- `[排序: 推文量↓]` indicator next to the header when `sort != SortNewestFirst`. Use 推文量 in the indicator since that's the user's wording, even though the field is `recommend_score` (note the trade-off in a code comment).
- Footer hint adds `· / search · s sort · ? help`.

Concurrent-broadcast race:
- `ArticleAddedMsg` arriving for this board while a filter is active should respect the filter (just call `reload(anchorID)` — the new helper already passes m.filter). If the new article doesn't match the filter, it just won't appear, which is fine. If the user is typing (`searchActive`), defer the reload until they confirm? — Recommend: still reload silently (the textinput's own ticker won't be disturbed; only `m.articles` changes). The cursor will get re-anchored.

**`internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go`** (extend; current at screen_board_view_test.go:1)

New tests:
- `TestBoardView_SlashEntersSearchMode` + `TestBoardView_SearchPreventsActionKeys` (s, M, p, b, B all type into the input).
- `TestBoardView_FilterByTitle` — seed five articles with mixed titles, type `irst` + enter, expect only the `first` row.
- `TestBoardView_SortToggle_ByScore` — seed three articles, push them to scores 0/3/-1, press `s`, expect order `3, 0, -1` (skipping pinned).
- `TestBoardView_SortToggle_PinnedStillFirst` — pin a 0-score article, ensure it's first even with score sort active.
- `TestBoardView_SortPersistsAcrossReload` — press `s`, then receive `ArticleAddedMsg`, confirm the new ordering is preserved.
- `TestBoardView_FilterAndSort_Combined`.
- `TestBoardView_FilterCursorReanchorsByID` — after filter narrows results, the cursor stays on the same article ID when possible.

### 4. Footer hint + help.go updates

**`internal/tui/screen_board_list.go:104`** — append `· / search · ? help` to the help-line.

**`internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:244, 246`** — append `· / search · s sort · ? help` (and the variant for guest at 246).

**`internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:634`** — append `· ? help`. (No new keys, just the discoverability fix.)

**`internal/tui/help.go:43-49`** (ScreenBoardList block) — add:
```
{"/", "search boards (Esc clears, Enter confirms)"},
{"Esc (filtered)", "clear filter"},
```

**`internal/tui/help.go:51-63`** (ScreenBoardView block) — add:
```
{"/", "search articles by title"},
{"s", "toggle sort (Newest ↔ 推文量)"},
{"Esc (filtered)", "clear filter"},
```

`screenHelp[ScreenArticleView]` already lists every key; no schema change needed there. Globals at help.go:173 already advertise `?`.

**`internal/tui/help_test.go`** — extend whatever parity table tests already exist (see help_test.go) to assert the new entries are reachable on those screens.

## Trade-offs / alternatives considered

- **Why not a `push_count` column?** Cleaner semantics for "推文量". Costs: schema migration (0009-something), `pushes.go` mutation now has to update a counter, an existing test must be re-baselined. The cached `recommend_score` column already exists and does the job. If a future product decision says "score and push-count differ — score subtracts 噓 but 推文量 should count noise", we add the column then. Logged in code as a `// TODO(score-vs-pushcount)` near the new sort branch.
- **Server vs. client filter for articles:** server. The current 100-row cap means client filter would sometimes show 0 hits despite matches existing. SQLite + `WHERE board_id = ?` keeps the LIKE scan O(per-board article count) which is bounded.
- **Why `/` and not `f`?** `f` is not currently used either, but `/` is the universal vim convention and matches user mental model. `f` could be added as an alias.
- **Footer-only indicator vs. modal banner:** footer keeps the article count stable, which matters because list height is part of the layout (banner block at top, articles in middle, hint at bottom). A floating banner would shift everything.
- **Help-key perception fix without keymap change:** the user's complaint is real even though the keybinding works — they just couldn't see `?` on the screens they tried (board list, board view, article view). Pure footer-text fix.

## Risks

- **Cursor invalidation:** filter narrowing will leave `m.cursor` past the end of the slice if not clamped. Both screens need explicit `m.cursor = clamp(m.cursor, 0, len(filtered)-1)` after every reload/filter.
- **Concurrent broadcast during search:** `ArticleAddedMsg` arrives while user is typing. Recommended: still reload (silently honoring m.filter); searchActive textinput is unaffected. Document this in a code comment alongside the helper.
- **CJK width in textinput:** `bubbles/textinput` itself handles CJK reasonably, but `m.title.Width` (post-compose pattern, screen_post_compose.go:62) sets a width budget — apply the same trick: `m.search.Width = max(20, m.width - 12)` at WindowSizeMsg and on first render.
- **`strings.ToLower` for CJK in board filter:** no-op for CJK, fine for ASCII. If we ever add accented Latin board names, switch to `strings.EqualFold` over normalized runes — out of scope now.
- **`enter` overload:** in board list/view, `enter` currently means "open cursored item". When `searchActive`, `enter` must mean "confirm filter". The state machine handles this — cleanly gated by `searchActive`.
- **Backward-compat of ListByBoard shim:** must remain identical in the limit-only case so the existing `TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst`, `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst`, `TestArticles_ListByBoard_Limit` tests pass without modification.
- **Pinned-first interaction with score sort:** if user pins a -10 score article, it stays at top in score mode. This is the right call (pin is mod-explicit) but worth a code comment explaining the override.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go
- /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles_test.go

- /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/help.go
```

Design a plan for adding board/article search + article sort to a Go SSH BBS at /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs.

## User request (zh-TW, paraphrased)
1. Search boards on the board list screen (filter by typed substring)
2. Search articles on the board view screen (filter by title substring)
3. Sort articles by 推文量 (push amount / popularity) — current default is newest-first
4. Verify the `?` help key works (user thinks it doesn't); fix the perception/UX gap

## Current state (already explored)

### Help system — actually wired correctly
- `internal/tui/root.go:81-101` is the global key interceptor: catches `?` and toggles `m.helpVisible`, gated by `helpAvailable()`.
- `internal/tui/help.go` (254 lines) defines per-screen keymaps in the `screenHelp` map (lines 33–169). Globals at 173–177 advertise `?`, Ctrl+U, Ctrl+C.
- `helpAvailable()` at help.go:199-204 returns false on form screens (compose, register, password-change) — by design so `?` types literally.
- **Footer-hint gap**:
  - `screen_main_menu.go:122` advertises `? help` ✓
  - `screen_board_list.go:104` does NOT mention `?` ✗
  - `screen_article_view.go:634` does NOT mention `?` ✗

### Board list: `internal/tui/screen_board_list.go`
- Model: `boardListModel{deps, boards []*store.Board, cursor int, width/height, loadErr}`
- Loads via `deps.Store.Boards().List(ctx)` — sorted by name COLLATE NOCASE
- Keys: j/k/up/down, g/home/end, esc/h/← back, enter/space/l/→ open, Q quit
- ~108 lines

### Board view (article list): `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`
- Model: `boardViewModel{deps, board, articles, cursor, width/height, loadErr}` plus pin/comment-mode helpers
- Loads via `Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)` — hardcoded ORDER BY pinned-first, created_at DESC
- Has `findCursorByID()` re-anchor pattern (lines 52–77)
- Keys: j/k/up/down, g/home/end, enter/space/l/→ open, M (pin), B/b (banner), p (post), esc/h/← back, Q
- ~335 lines

### Store layer
- `internal/store/articles.go:69-94` — `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` returns `[]*Article`
- `articles` schema has `title`, `recommend_score` (cached signed score), `pinned_at`, `created_at`, `comments_mode`, etc. **No `push_count` column.**
- `internal/store/boards.go:42-58` — `List(ctx)` returns all boards sorted by name
- Driver: `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go, no FTS5)
- Index `idx_articles_board_created` on `(board_id, created_at DESC)`. No title index.
- Tests in `articles_test.go`, `boards_test.go` cover existing sort guarantees.

### Other patterns
- `bubbles/textinput` is used in `screen_post_compose.go`, `screen_mail.go`, `screen_register.go`, push input in `screen_article_view.go`. Established pattern: dedicated textinput field, Update() delegates.
- `internal/tui/runewidth.go` has `PadRight`, `Truncate`, `Width` for CJK-safe rendering.
- CLAUDE.md is strict: form screens skip h/l (reserved for text editing). Search input mode means `h` and `l` must NOT navigate while typing.
- `Store.writeMu` is process-wide; reads don't need it.

## Design constraints / decisions to make

Please produce a concrete plan that addresses:

1. **Search-mode UX on list screens**:
   - Recommend a key (`/` is the obvious vim convention; the codebase isn't using it elsewhere)
   - State machine: idle → searching (textinput active, filtered list visible, h/l/j/k disabled or gated) → confirmed (Enter — keep filter, return to nav) → cleared (Esc from filtered nav state)
   - Where to render the input: top of screen vs footer? Footer seems best to preserve the row layout.
   - How to indicate active filter when not in search mode (e.g. `[搜尋: foo · 12 results]` indicator)
   - Confirm h/l vim keys must NOT trigger nav while in search mode (they should type into textinput) — match the form-screen rule

2. **Boards: client-side vs server-side filter?**
   - Boards are typically <100; client-side filter is simpler, no DB change. Recommend this.
   - If server-side, would need a new `Boards.Search(ctx, query)` method.

3. **Articles: client-side vs server-side filter?**
   - Currently load 100; if user types a substring not in those 100, they get nothing.
   - Recommend server-side: extend `ListByBoard` to take a title-LIKE filter (with COLLATE NOCASE), bound by the same limit. LIKE '%query%' won't use indexes, but board_id WHERE clause keeps scan small per-board.
   - API design: do we add `ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, opts ListOpts)` and have `ListByBoard` delegate to it, or add a separate `SearchByTitle` method? Recommend the options struct — it scales to support sort param too.

4. **Sort by what field?**
   - User said 推文量 (push amount). The cached `articles.recommend_score` is the signed score (+1/-1 from each push), which is *related* but not literal count.
   - Option A: Sort by `recommend_score DESC` — no schema change, semantically "popular/hot". This is how PTT 熱門看板 often works.
   - Option B: Add a `push_count` column maintained by PushRepo alongside recommend_score, sort by that.
   - Recommend A (lower-risk, no migration), but call out the trade-off in the plan.

5. **Sort toggle key & UX**:
   - `s` is unbound in board view — propose press `s` to cycle: `Newest → Score → Newest…`
   - Pinned articles still float to top in both modes
   - Show current mode in the footer
   - Persist per-screen-instance only (don't persist across sessions for now)

6. **Help-key footer fix**:
   - Add `? help` to board list, board view, article view footers.
   - Add new keys (`/`, `s`) to `help.go` `screenHelp` entries for those screens.
   - Form screens stay as-is (no `?` advertised, since suppressed by helpAvailable()).

7. **Tests** to add (extend existing files):
   - `articles_test.go`: TitleSearch, SortByScore, Search+Sort combined, pinned-first preserved across both sorts
   - `boards_test.go`: nothing if filter is client-side
   - `screen_board_list_test.go` (create if absent): search mode entry/exit, filter results, cursor preservation
   - `screen_board_view_test.go`: same + sort toggle
   - Keybinding-parity table tests should be extended as CLAUDE.md mentions

## What to deliver

A step-by-step implementation plan with:
- File-by-file changes (paths and line ranges)
- New files to create (if any)
- Test cases to add
- Order of work (start with store layer; then board list; then board view; then footers/help.go)
- Trade-offs and alternatives considered for the design decisions above
- Risks (e.g. cursor invalidation when filter narrows/widens; race with concurrent broadcasts; CJK width in search input)

Length: ~600 words is fine. This is a planning doc, not a tutorial.

53				}
54			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
55				if len(m.boards) == 0 {
56					return m, nil
57				}
58				b := m.boards[m.cursor]
59				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: b.ID} }
60			}
61		}
62		return m, nil
63	}
64	
65	func (m boardListModel) View() string {
66		var b strings.Builder
67		b.WriteString("\n")
68		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板列表 Boards "))
69		b.WriteString("\n\n")
70	
71		if m.loadErr != nil {
72			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ load failed: "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
73			return b.String()
74		}
75		if len(m.boards) == 0 {
76			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no boards yet)") + "\n")
77			return b.String()
78		}
79	
80		const (
81			nameW    = 14
82			titleW   = 32
83		)
84		header := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s  %s",
85			PadRight("Name", nameW),
86			PadRight("Title", titleW),
87			"Description",
88		)
89		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
90	
91		for i, br := range m.boards {
92			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s  %s",
93				PadRight(br.Name, nameW),
94				PadRight(br.Title, titleW),
95				Truncate(br.Description, max(20, m.width-nameW-titleW-8)),
96			)
97			if i == m.cursor {
98				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
99			} else {
100				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
101			}
102		}
103	
104		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
105		b.WriteString("\n")
106		return b.String()
107	}
108

1	package tui

3	import (
4		"context"

8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	type boardViewModel struct {
14		deps     Deps
15		board    *store.Board
16		articles []*store.Article
17		cursor   int
18		width    int
19		height   int
20		loadErr  error
21	}
22	
23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case ArticlePinChangedMsg:
49			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
50				// Pin re-orders the list. Re-anchor the cursor by article ID so
51				// the same article stays highlighted across the reflow.
52				anchorID := int64(0)
53				if m.cursor < len(m.articles) {
54					anchorID = m.articles[m.cursor].ID
55				}
56				ctx := context.Background()
57				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
58					m.articles = arts
59					m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID)
60				}
61			}
62			return m, nil
63		case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
64			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
65				// Mode flip doesn't reorder, but we still re-anchor the cursor
66				// for parity with the pin handler — if a future change adds
67				// list ordering by comments_mode, we won't lose the highlight.
68				anchorID := int64(0)
69				if m.cursor < len(m.articles) {
70					anchorID = m.articles[m.cursor].ID
71				}
72				ctx := context.Background()
73				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
74					m.articles = arts
75					m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID)
76				}
77			}
78			return m, nil
79		case tea.KeyMsg:
80			switch msg.String() {
81			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
82				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
83			case "Q":
84				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
85			case "up", "k", "[":
86				if m.cursor > 0 {
87					m.cursor--
88				}
89			case "down", "j", "]":
90				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
91					m.cursor++
92				}
93			case "home", "g":
94				m.cursor = 0
95			case "end", "G":
96				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
97					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
98				}
99			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
100				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
101					return m, nil
102				}
103				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
104				return m, func() tea.Msg {
105					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
106				}
107			case "p":
108				if m.board == nil {
109					return m, nil
110				}
111				return m, func() tea.Msg {
112					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
113				}
114			case "b":
115				if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
116					return m, nil
117				}
118				return m, func() tea.Msg {
119					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardSplash, BoardID: m.board.ID}
120				}
121			case "B":
122				if m.board == nil || !m.canEditBanner() {
123					return m, nil
124				}
125				return m, func() tea.Msg {
126					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardBannerEdit, BoardID: m.board.ID}
127				}
128			case "M":
129				// Toggle pin on the cursor article. Mod+ only — silent no-op
130				// otherwise so non-mods don't get a confusing error toast for
131				// trying a key the help line doesn't even mention to them.
132				if m.board == nil || !m.canPin() || len(m.articles) == 0 {
133					return m, nil
134				}
135				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
136				pinned := !a.PinnedAt.Valid // toggle
137				ctx := context.Background()
138				u := m.deps.User
139				if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, u.ID, u.Role, pinned); err != nil {
140					return m, nil
141				}
142				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
143					m.articles = arts
144					m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, a.ID)
145				}
146				if m.deps.Broker != nil {
147					m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticlePinChangedMsg{
148						BoardID: m.board.ID, ArticleID: a.ID, Pinned: pinned,
149					})
150				}
151				return m, nil
152			}
153		}
154		return m, nil
155	}
156	
157	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
158		var b strings.Builder
159		b.WriteString("\n")
160		if m.board != nil {
161			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
162		} else {
163			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
164		}
165		b.WriteString("\n\n")
166		b.WriteString(m.renderBanner())
167	
168		if m.loadErr != nil {
169			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
170			return b.String()
171		}
172		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
173			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
174			help := "p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
175			if m.isGuest() {
176				hint = "(no articles yet)"
177				help = "Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
178			}
179			help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
180			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
181			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
182			return b.String()
183		}
184	
185		const (
186			idxW    = 4
187			dateW   = 11
188			scoreW  = 4
189			authorW = 14
190		)
191		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
192		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
193			PadRight("#", idxW),
194			PadRight("Date", dateW),
195			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
196			PadRight("Author", authorW),
197			PadRight("Title", titleW),
198		)
199		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
200	
201		for i, a := range m.articles {
202			score := ""
203			switch {
204			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
205				score = "爆"
206			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
207				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
208			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
209				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
210			}
211			// Pinned (板規 / 置頂) articles surface a leading "[M] " marker —
212			// the same glyph PTT uses for M-marked posts. Locked / arrows-only
213			// articles stack additional [鎖] / [箭] badges so users see the
214			// constraint before opening the article. Long titles still get
215			// truncated by Truncate.
216			title := a.Title
217			var prefix string
218			if a.PinnedAt.Valid {
219				prefix += "[M]"
220			}
221			switch a.CommentsMode {
222			case store.CommentsModeLocked:
223				prefix += "[鎖]"
224			case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
225				prefix += "[箭]"
226			}
227			if prefix != "" {
228				title = prefix + " " + a.Title
229			}
230			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
231				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
232				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
233				PadRight(score, scoreW),
234				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
235				Truncate(title, titleW),
236			)
237			if i == m.cursor {
238				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
239			} else {
240				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
241			}
242		}
243	
244		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
245		if m.isGuest() {
246			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
247		}
248		help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
249		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
250		b.WriteString("\n")
251		return b.String()
252	}
253	
254	func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
255		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
256	}
257	
258	func (m boardViewModel) canEditBanner() bool {
259		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
260	}
261	
262	// canPin gates the M shortcut. Mirrors canEditBanner — pinning is a
263	// moderation action and intentionally does NOT admit the article author
264	// (an author shouldn't bubble their own post to the top).
265	func (m boardViewModel) canPin() bool {
266		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
267	}
268	
269	// findCursorByID returns the index of the article with the given ID in
270	// the slice, or 0 if not found. Used to re-anchor the cursor after a
271	// pin/unpin reorders the list.
272	func findCursorByID(arts []*store.Article, id int64) int {
273		for i, a := range arts {
274			if a.ID == id {
275				return i
276			}
277		}
278		return 0
279	}
280	
281	// appendBannerHelp tacks "b banner" / "B edit" / "M pin" hints onto a
282	// help-line only when relevant: `b` requires a non-empty banner; `B` and
283	// `M` require mod+. Order matches the existing pattern (action keys
284	// before navigation). Name kept for git-blame stability — it now covers
285	// all mod-affordances on the board view, not just banner ones.
286	func (m boardViewModel) appendBannerHelp(help string) string {
287		if m.board != nil && m.board.Banner != "" {
288			help += " · b banner"
289		}
290		if m.canEditBanner() {
291			help += " · B edit"
292		}
293		if m.canPin() {
294			help += " · M pin/unpin"
295		}
296		return help
297	}
298	
299	const maxInlineBannerLines = 8
300	
301	// renderBanner produces the inline banner block shown above the article
302	// list. Empty string when the board has no banner. Each line is
303	// ANSI-aware-truncated to the viewport width with a 2-space left margin;
304	// the block is capped at maxInlineBannerLines with a "(press b)" hint
305	// when the source has more.
306	func (m boardViewModel) renderBanner() string {
307		if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
308			return ""
309		}
310		width := m.width - 2
311		if width < 20 {
312			// Before the first WindowSizeMsg lands m.width is 0; fall back to a
313			// generous default so the banner doesn't render as a single column.
314			width = 78
315		}
316		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimRight(m.board.Banner, "\n"), "\n")
317		limit := maxInlineBannerLines
318		truncated := false
319		if len(lines) > limit {
320			lines = lines[:limit]
321			truncated = true
322		}
323		var out strings.Builder
324		for _, line := range lines {
325			out.WriteString("  ")
326			out.WriteString(bannerTruncate(line, width))
327			out.WriteString("\n")
328		}
329		if truncated {
330			out.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("…(banner truncated — press b for full view)") + "\n")
331		}
332		out.WriteString("\n")
333		return out.String()
334	}

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
8	)
9	
10	// HelpEntry is one row in a screen's keymap. Keys is the displayed shortcut
11	// (e.g. "Enter/→/l"), Desc the human-readable action.
12	type HelpEntry struct {
13		Keys string
14		Desc string
15	}
16	
17	// helpHeader is the optional title row shown above the keymap. Empty for
18	// most screens; some screens use it to clarify mode-specific keys.
19	type helpSection struct {
20		Heading string
21		Entries []HelpEntry
22	}
23	
24	// screenHelp maps a Screen constant to the keys we want the help overlay
25	// to surface. Mirrors the inline help-line strings rendered at the bottom
26	// of each screen, with extra notes on globals (Ctrl+U, Ctrl+C, ?) so the
27	// overlay is self-contained.
28	//
29	// Form screens (compose, edit, password change, board-banner edit) are
30	// intentionally absent: they need to pass `?` through to textinput so the
31	// user can type it as a literal character. Their bottom help-line already
32	// surfaces every key they bind.
33	var screenHelp = map[Screen][]helpSection{
34		ScreenMainMenu: {{
35			Heading: "Main menu",
36			Entries: []HelpEntry{
37				{"↑/↓ j/k", "move cursor"},

98				},
99			},
100			{
101				Heading: "Scrollback focused (after Tab)",
102				Entries: []HelpEntry{

104					{"g / G / Home / End", "top / end"},
105					{"Tab", "back to input"},

107					{"Q", "back to main menu"},
108				},
109			},
110		},
111		ScreenOnline: {{
112			Heading: "Online users",
113			Entries: []HelpEntry{
114				{"↑/↓ j/k [/]", "move cursor"},
115				{"Enter/→/l", "丟水球 (compose)"},
116				{"t", "對話 thread (chat-style)"},
117				{"g / G / Home / End", "top / bottom"},
118				{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
119				{"Q", "back to main menu"},
120			},
121		}},

123			Heading: "信箱 (mail inbox)",
124			Entries: []HelpEntry{
125				{"↑/↓ j/k [/]", "move cursor"},
126				{"Enter/→/l", "open thread"},
127				{"r", "reply to cursored mail"},
128				{"c", "compose new mail"},
129				{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
130				{"Q", "back to main menu"},
131			},

133		ScreenMailThread: {{

135			Entries: []HelpEntry{
136				{"↑/↓ j/k", "scroll"},
137				{"g", "top"},
138				{"r", "reply"},
139				{"Esc/←/h", "back to inbox"},
140				{"Q", "back to main menu"},
141			},
142		}},
143		ScreenAdminUsers: {{
144			Heading: "Admin: users",
145			Entries: []HelpEntry{
146				{"↑/↓ j/k", "move cursor"},
147				{"[ / ]", "prev / next page"},
148				{"a / m / u / g", "promote to admin / mod / user / guest"},
149				{"R", "reset password (forces change on next login)"},
150				{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
151			},
152		}},
153		ScreenArticleExport: {{
154			Heading: "Export article",
155			Entries: []HelpEntry{
156				{"1", "plain body"},
157				{"2", "body + pushes"},
158				{"3", "save to data/exports/<userid>/"},
159				{"c", "OSC52 copy to clipboard"},
160				{"Esc/←/h/q", "back"},
161			},
162		}},
163		ScreenBoardSplash: {{
164			Heading: "Board banner splash",
165			Entries: []HelpEntry{
166				{"any key", "return to board view"},
167			},
168		}},
169	}
170	
171	// helpGlobals are the cross-screen keys shown on every help page so users
172	// don't need to discover them per screen.
173	var helpGlobals = []HelpEntry{
174		{"?", "show this help (any key dismisses)"},
175		{"Ctrl+U", "open 水球 inbox (logged-in only)"},
176		{"Ctrl+C", "disconnect"},
177	}
178	
179	// isFormScreen reports whether a screen is a text-entry form whose
180	// textinput / textarea must receive '?' as a literal character. The help
181	// overlay is suppressed on these screens; the bottom help-line each form
182	// already renders is the source of truth there.
183	func isFormScreen(s Screen) bool {
184		switch s {
185		case ScreenPostCompose,
186			ScreenWBCompose,
187			ScreenMailCompose,
188			ScreenArticleEdit,
189			ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
190			ScreenPasswordChange:
191			return true
192		}
193		return false
194	}
195	
196	// helpAvailable reports whether the help overlay should respond to '?' on
197	// this screen. False for form screens and during register/MustChangePassword
198	// (those flows start before any Screen const is meaningful).
199	func helpAvailable(deps Deps, s Screen) bool {
200		if deps.IsRegister || deps.MustChangePassword {
201			return false
202		}
203		return !isFormScreen(s)
204	}
205	
206	// helpBox is the lipgloss frame around the help body. Width is set per-call
207	// so the box hugs the rendered content rather than spanning the terminal.
208	var helpBox = lipgloss.NewStyle().
209		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
210		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("63")).
211		Padding(1, 2)
212	
213	// renderHelp builds the full-screen help overlay for the given screen.
214	// Includes per-screen entries (or a fallback message) plus the globals.
215	func renderHelp(s Screen) string {
216		var b strings.Builder
217		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 鍵盤捷徑說明 Help ") + "\n\n")
218	
219		sections, ok := screenHelp[s]
220		if !ok {
221			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no per-screen keymap registered for this screen)") + "\n\n")
222		} else {
223			for i, sec := range sections {
224				if i > 0 {
225					b.WriteString("\n")
226				}
227				if sec.Heading != "" {
228					b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  "+sec.Heading) + "\n")
229				}
230				for _, e := range sec.Entries {
231					b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
232				}
233			}
234			b.WriteString("\n")
235		}
236	
237		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  Global") + "\n")
238		for _, e := range helpGlobals {
239			b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
240		}
241	
242		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to dismiss"))
243	
244		return helpBox.Render(b.String())
245	}
246	
247	// formatHelpRow pads keys to a stable column width so the descriptions
248	// align — uses the runewidth-aware PadRight because keys often contain
249	// arrows / CJK glyphs.
250	func formatHelpRow(e HelpEntry) string {
251		const keysW = 22
252		return fmt.Sprintf("    %s  %s", PadRight(e.Keys, keysW), e.Desc)
253	}
254
```

10	// CommentsMode controls who can leave 推/噓/→ pushes on an article. The
11	// values are persisted as TEXT in articles.comments_mode (see migration
12	// 0008). States are mutually exclusive — 'locked' is strictly stronger
13	// than 'arrows_only', so a single-column enum keeps the precedence rule
14	// unambiguous. Mod-only mutator: ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
15	type CommentsMode string
16	
17	const (
18		CommentsModeOpen       CommentsMode = "open"        // default — push, boo, arrow all allowed
19		CommentsModeArrowsOnly CommentsMode = "arrows_only" // only neutral arrow comments
20		CommentsModeLocked     CommentsMode = "locked"      // all pushes rejected
21	)
22	
23	func (m CommentsMode) Valid() bool {
24		switch m {
25		case CommentsModeOpen, CommentsModeArrowsOnly, CommentsModeLocked:
26			return true
27		}
28		return false
29	}

31	type Article struct {
32		ID             int64
33		BoardID        int64
34		AuthorID       int64
35		AuthorUserID   string
36		Title          string
37		Body           string
38		RecommendScore int64
39		Filemode       int64
40		CreatedAt      time.Time
41		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
42		PinnedAt       sql.NullTime
43		CommentsMode   CommentsMode
44	}
45	
46	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
47	
48	var (
49		ErrArticleNotFound       = errors.New("article not found")
50		ErrPermissionDenied      = errors.New("permission denied")
51		ErrInvalidCommentsMode   = errors.New("invalid comments mode")
52	)
53	
54	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
55		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at, comments_mode`
56	
57	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
58		a := &Article{}
59		var commentsMode string
60		err := row.Scan(
61			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
62			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
63			&commentsMode,
64		)
65		a.CommentsMode = CommentsMode(commentsMode)
66		return a, err
67	}

69	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
70		if limit <= 0 {
71			limit = 50
72		}
73		// Pinned articles surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1);
74		// within each group, newest first by created_at then id (the latter
75		// disambiguates rows that share a second-resolution timestamp).
76		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
77			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ?
78			 ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
79			boardID, limit,
80		)
81		if err != nil {
82			return nil, err
83		}
84		defer rows.Close()
85		var out []*Article
86		for rows.Next() {
87			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
88			if err != nil {
89				return nil, err
90			}
91			out = append(out, a)
92		}
93		return out, rows.Err()
94	}
