96	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
97		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
98		a, err := scanArticle(row)
99		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
100			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
101		}
102		return a, err
103	}
104	
105	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
106	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
107	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
108	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
109	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
110		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
111			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
112			boardID, articleID,
113		)
114		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
115			return 0, 0, scanErr
116		}
117		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
118			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
119			boardID, articleID,
120		)
121		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
122			return 0, 0, scanErr
123		}
124		return prev, next, nil
125	}
126	
127	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
128		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
129		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
130		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
131			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
132			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
133		)
134		if err != nil {
135			return nil, err
136		}
137		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
138		if err != nil {
139			return nil, err
140		}
141		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
142		return scanArticle(row)
143	}
144	
145	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
146	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
147	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
148	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
149	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
150		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
151		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
152		var authorID int64
153		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
154			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
155		).Scan(&authorID)
156		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
157			return ErrArticleNotFound
158		}
159		if err != nil {
160			return err
161		}
162		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
163			return ErrPermissionDenied
164		}
165		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
166		return err
167	}
168	
169	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
170	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
171	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
172	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
173	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
174	// edit flow.
175	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
176		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
177		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
178		var authorID int64
179		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
180			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
181		).Scan(&authorID)
182		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
183			return ErrArticleNotFound
184		}
185		if err != nil {
186			return err
187		}
188		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
189			return ErrPermissionDenied
190		}
191		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
192			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
193			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
194		)
195		return err
196	}
197	
198	// SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission
199	// shape mirrors BoardRepo.UpdateBanner: requesterRole must be at-or-above
200	// mod. Pinning is a moderation action — unlike Update/Delete it does NOT
201	// admit the article author. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row is gone,
202	// ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
203	//
204	// Multi-pin per board is supported: the call simply sets pinned_at; nothing
205	// rejects a second pinned row on the same board.
206	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error {
207		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
208			return ErrPermissionDenied
209		}
210		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
211		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
212		var exists int64
213		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
214			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
215		).Scan(&exists)
216		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
217			return ErrArticleNotFound
218		}
219		if err != nil {
220			return err
221		}
222		if pinned {
223			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
224				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
225			)
226		} else {
227			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
228				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = NULL WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
229			)
230		}
231		return err
232	}
233	
234	// SetCommentsMode flips an article's comments_mode (open / arrows_only /
235	// locked). Permission shape mirrors SetPinned: requesterRole must be
236	// at-or-above mod, and unlike Update/Delete this does NOT admit the
237	// article author — locking comments is a moderation action. Returns
238	// ErrInvalidCommentsMode if mode is not one of the three constants,
239	// ErrPermissionDenied / ErrArticleNotFound otherwise. requesterID is
240	// accepted for signature parity with the other mutators (Pin/Update/Delete)
241	// but unused for the gate.
242	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetCommentsMode(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, mode CommentsMode) error {
243		if !mode.Valid() {
244			return ErrInvalidCommentsMode
245		}
246		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
247			return ErrPermissionDenied
248		}
249		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
250		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
251		var exists int64
252		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
253			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
254		).Scan(&exists)
255		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
256			return ErrArticleNotFound
257		}
258		if err != nil {
259			return err
260		}
261		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
262			`UPDATE articles SET comments_mode = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(mode), articleID,
263		)
264		return err
265	}

78	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
79	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
80	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
81		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
82		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
83		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
84			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
85				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
86				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
87				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')

92	// UpdateBanner sets the banner column on a board. Permission: requester
93	// must have a role at-or-above mod (the global mod role; per-board BM is
94	// not consulted). Returns ErrBoardNotFound if the board is missing,
95	// ErrPermissionDenied if the requester is unauthorized.
96	//
97	// Mirrors ArticleRepo.Update's permission shape; takes writeMu for the
98	// same reason (single-statement write is fine, but keeping the lock
99	// discipline consistent across repos is the point).
100	func (r *BoardRepo) UpdateBanner(ctx context.Context, boardID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, banner string) error {
101		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
102			return ErrPermissionDenied
103		}
104		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
105		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
106		var exists int64

108		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
109			return ErrBoardNotFound
110		}
111		if err != nil {
112			return err
113		}
114		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE boards SET banner = ? WHERE id = ?`, banner, boardID)

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
15	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
16	type Deps struct {
17		Store              *store.Store
18		Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
19		User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
20		IsRegister         bool
21		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
25	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
26	// and the toast footer.
27	type Root struct {
28		deps   Deps
29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	
36		// activeScreen tracks which Screen the current sub corresponds to, so
37		// the help overlay can render a context-aware keymap. Set in NewRoot
38		// for the bootstrap sub and in navigate for every subsequent swap.
39		activeScreen Screen
40		// helpVisible is true while the help overlay should render in place of
41		// the sub view. Toggled on '?' and dismissed by any key.
42		helpVisible bool
43	}
44	
45	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
46	
47	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
48	//
49	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
50	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
51	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
52	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
53		r := Root{deps: deps}
54		switch {
55		case deps.IsRegister:
56			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
57			// No Screen const for register; help is suppressed via deps.IsRegister.
58		case deps.MustChangePassword:
59			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
60			r.activeScreen = ScreenPasswordChange
61		default:
62			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
63			r.activeScreen = ScreenMainMenu
64		}
65		return r
66	}
67	
68	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
69		return m.sub.Init()
70	}
71	
72	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
73		switch msg := msg.(type) {
74		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
75			m.width = msg.Width
76			m.height = msg.Height
77			var cmd tea.Cmd
78			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
79			return m, cmd
80	
81		case tea.KeyMsg:
82			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
83			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
84				return m, tea.Quit
85			}
86			// Help overlay: any key dismisses, swallow the keypress so the
87			// sub doesn't act on it. ctrl+c above is the deliberate exception.
88			if m.helpVisible {
89				m.helpVisible = false
90				return m, nil
91			}
92			// '?' opens the help overlay on screens that aren't typing-forms.
93			if msg.String() == "?" && helpAvailable(m.deps, m.activeScreen) {
94				m.helpVisible = true
95				return m, nil
96			}
97			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
98			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
99				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
100			}
101	
102		case NavigateMsg:
103			return m.navigate(msg)
104	
105		case ErrorMsg:
106			if msg.Err != nil {
107				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
108			}
109			return m, nil
110	
111		case WBIncomingMsg:
112			// Suppress the toast when the active screen is a wbThreadModel
113			// pointed at this exact counterparty — the thread itself appends
114			// the message, so the toast would be redundant noise.
115			showToast := true
116			if m.activeScreen == ScreenWBThread {
117				if tm, ok := m.sub.(wbThreadModel); ok && tm.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
118					showToast = false
119				}
120			}
121			if showToast {
122				m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
123			}
124			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect. (The
125			// thread also marks it read — both paths are idempotent.)
126			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
127				go func(id int64) {
128					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
129				}(msg.ID)
130			}
131			// fall through so the active screen can also react
132		}
133	
134		var cmd tea.Cmd
135		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
136		return m, cmd
137	}
138	
139	func (m Root) View() string {
140		if m.helpVisible {
141			// Replace the sub view entirely so the keymap reads cleanly. Toast
142			// suppressed too — would distract from a help screen.
143			return renderHelp(m.activeScreen)
144		}
145		v := m.sub.View()
146		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
147			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
148		}
149		return v
150	}
151	
152	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
153		m.toast = s
154		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
155	}
156	
157	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
158	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
159	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
160		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
161			return nil
162		}
163		switch to {
164		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
165			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
166		}
167		return nil
168	}
169	
170	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
171	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
172	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
173		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
174			return nil
175		}
176		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
177			return nil
178		}
179		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
180	}
181	
182	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
183		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
184			return m, cmd
185		}
186		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
187			return m, cmd
188		}
189		var sub tea.Model
190		switch n.To {
191		case ScreenMainMenu:
192			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
193		case ScreenBoardList:
194			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
195		case ScreenBoardView:
196			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
197		case ScreenArticleView:
198			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
199		case ScreenPostCompose:
200			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
201		case ScreenWBInbox:
202			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
203		case ScreenWBCompose:
204			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
205		case ScreenWBThread:
206			sub = newWBThreadModel(m.deps, n.CounterpartyUserID)
207		case ScreenOnline:
208			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
209		case ScreenMailInbox:
210			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
211		case ScreenMailThread:
212			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
213		case ScreenMailCompose:
214			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
215		case ScreenPasswordChange:
216			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
217		case ScreenAdminUsers:
218			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
219		case ScreenArticleEdit:
220			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
221		case ScreenArticleExport:
222			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
223		case ScreenBoardSplash:
224			sub = newBoardSplashModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
225		case ScreenBoardBannerEdit:
226			sub = newBoardBannerEditModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
227		default:
228			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
229			return m, nil
230		}
231		m.sub = sub
232		m.activeScreen = n.To
233		cmd := sub.Init()
234		if m.width > 0 {
235			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
236			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
237			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
238			if cmd2 != nil {
239				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
240			}
241		}
242		return m, cmd
243	}
244
```

98	func TestArticles_NeighboursOf(t *testing.T) {
99		ctx := context.Background()
100		st := storetest.New(t)
101		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
102		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")

104	
105		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "first", "")
106		b, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "second", "")
107		c, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "third", "")
108	
109		// Middle article: both neighbours present.
110		prev, next, err := st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, b.ID)
111		if err != nil {
112			t.Fatalf("middle: %v", err)
113		}
114		if prev != a.ID || next != c.ID {
115			t.Errorf("middle: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=%d next=%d", prev, next, a.ID, c.ID)
116		}
117	
118		// First article: prev=0, next=second.
119		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, a.ID)
120		if prev != 0 || next != b.ID {
121			t.Errorf("first: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=0 next=%d", prev, next, b.ID)
122		}
123	
124		// Last article: prev=second, next=0.
125		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, c.ID)
126		if prev != b.ID || next != 0 {
127			t.Errorf("last: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=%d next=0", prev, next, b.ID)
128		}
129	
130		// Wrong board: 0/0 even though the article exists elsewhere.
131		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, b.ID)
132		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
133			t.Errorf("wrong board: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
134		}
135	
136		// Single-article board: both 0. Use the empty `other` board for this.
137		d, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, other.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "alone", "")
138		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, d.ID)
139		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
140			t.Errorf("single: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
141		}
142	}

336	func TestArticles_SetPinned_RequiresMod(t *testing.T) {
337		ctx := context.Background()
338		st := storetest.New(t)
339		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
340		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
341		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "rules", "body")
342	
343		// Author themselves cannot pin their own post — pinning is a moderation
344		// action and intentionally diverges from Update/Delete which admit author.
345		for _, role := range []store.Role{store.RoleGuest, store.RoleUser} {
346			t.Run("denied_"+string(role), func(t *testing.T) {
347				err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, user.ID, role, true)
348				if !errors.Is(err, store.ErrPermissionDenied) {
349					t.Errorf("got %v, want ErrPermissionDenied", err)
350				}
351				got, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
352				if got.PinnedAt.Valid {

468	func TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst(t *testing.T) {
469		ctx := context.Background()
470		st := storetest.New(t)
471		mod := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "mod", "")
472		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
473	
474		// Create four articles in chronological order. Pin the second one
475		// (oldest pin candidate that isn't the very first row, so we can prove
476		// it bubbles to the top regardless of created_at).
477		titles := []string{"first", "second", "third", "fourth"}
478		ids := make(map[string]int64, len(titles))
479		for _, ti := range titles {
480			a, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, mod.ID, mod.UserID, ti, "")
481			if err != nil {
482				t.Fatalf("create %q: %v", ti, err)
483			}
484			ids[ti] = a.ID
485			time.Sleep(15 * time.Millisecond)
486		}
487		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, ids["second"], mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
488			t.Fatalf("pin: %v", err)
489		}
490	
491		got, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
492		if err != nil {
493			t.Fatalf("ListByBoard: %v", err)
494		}
495		if len(got) != 4 {
496			t.Fatalf("got %d articles, want 4", len(got))
497		}
498		// Expected order: pinned first ("second"), then unpinned by created_at DESC.
499		want := []string{"second", "fourth", "third", "first"}
500		for i, w := range want {
501			if got[i].Title != w {
502				t.Errorf("[%d] = %q, want %q (full order: %v)", i, got[i].Title, w, titlesOf(got))
503			}
504		}
505	
506		// Pin a second article — both should now be at the top, ordered by
507		// created_at DESC within the pinned group.
508		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, ids["fourth"], mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
509			t.Fatalf("pin fourth: %v", err)
510		}
511		got, _ = st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
512		wantMulti := []string{"fourth", "second", "third", "first"}
513		for i, w := range wantMulti {
514			if got[i].Title != w {
515				t.Errorf("multi-pin [%d] = %q, want %q (full order: %v)", i, got[i].Title, w, titlesOf(got))
516			}
517		}
518	}
519	
520	func TestArticles_Update_PreservesPinnedAt(t *testing.T) {
521		ctx := context.Background()
522		st := storetest.New(t)
523		mod := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "mod", "")
524		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
525		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, mod.ID, mod.UserID, "rules v1", "body")
526		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
527			t.Fatalf("pin: %v", err)
528		}
529		pinnedBefore, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
530		if !pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Valid {
531			t.Fatalf("pin precondition failed")
532		}
533		if err := st.Articles().Update(ctx, a.ID, mod.ID, store.RoleMod, "rules v2", "amended body"); err != nil {
534			t.Fatalf("Update: %v", err)
535		}
536		got, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
537		if !got.PinnedAt.Valid {
538			t.Errorf("PinnedAt cleared by Update — must be preserved")
539		}
540		if !got.PinnedAt.Time.Equal(pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Time) {
541			t.Errorf("PinnedAt mutated by Update: before=%v after=%v", pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Time, got.PinnedAt.Time)
542		}
543		if got.Title != "rules v2" {
544			t.Errorf("Update did not apply: title=%q", got.Title)
545		}
546	}
547	
548	func titlesOf(arts []*store.Article) []string {
549		out := make([]string, len(arts))
550		for i, a := range arts {
551			out[i] = a.Title
552		}
553		return out
554	}

125	
126	func contains(s, sub string) bool {
127		return len(s) >= len(sub) && (s == sub || indexOf(s, sub) >= 0)
128	}
129	
130	func indexOf(s, sub string) int {
131		for i := 0; i+len(sub) <= len(s); i++ {
132			if s[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {
133				return i
134			}
135		}
136		return -1
137	}

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		composeFocusTitle = iota
16		composeFocusBody
17	)
18	
19	type postComposeModel struct {
20		deps    Deps
21		boardID int64
22		title   textinput.Model
23		body    textarea.Model
24		focus   int
25		width   int
26		height  int
27		err     string
28	}
29	
30	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
31		ti := textinput.New()
32		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
33		ti.CharLimit = 64
34		ti.Width = 60
35		ti.Focus()
36	
37		ta := textarea.New()
38		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
39		ta.CharLimit = 8000
40		ta.SetWidth(72)
41		ta.SetHeight(12)
42	
43		return postComposeModel{
44			deps:    deps,
45			boardID: boardID,
46			title:   ti,
47			body:    ta,
48			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
49		}
50	}
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
53	
54	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
55		switch msg := msg.(type) {
56		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
57			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
58			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
59			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
60			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
61			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
62			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
63			return m, nil
64	
65		case tea.KeyMsg:
66			switch msg.String() {
67			case "esc":
68				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
69			case "tab":
70				m.toggleFocus()
71				return m, nil
72			case "ctrl+s":
73				return m.submit()
74			case "ctrl+i":
75				m.importFromBody()
76				return m, nil
77			}
78		}
79	
80		var cmd tea.Cmd
81		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
82			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
83			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
84				m.toggleFocus()
85				return m, nil
86			}
87			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
88		} else {
89			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
90		}
91		return m, cmd
92	}
93	
94	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
95		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
96			m.focus = composeFocusBody
97			m.title.Blur()
98			m.body.Focus()
99		} else {
100			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
101			m.body.Blur()
102			m.title.Focus()
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// importFromBody parses the current body as markdown and prefills title +
107	// body from any frontmatter found. If the body has no frontmatter it's
108	// left alone (with a helpful error). Pushes in the parsed input are
109	// silently ignored — see plan: import via Ctrl+I always credits the
110	// current user as author and never restores foreign-authored pushes.
111	func (m *postComposeModel) importFromBody() {
112		raw := m.body.Value()
113		if strings.TrimSpace(raw) == "" {
114			m.err = "貼上 markdown（含 --- frontmatter ---）後再按 Ctrl+I"
115			return
116		}
117		parsed, err := markdown.Parse(raw)
118		if err != nil {
119			m.err = "parse: " + err.Error()
120			return
121		}
122		if parsed.Title == "" && parsed.Body == strings.TrimRight(raw, "\n") {
123			// Parser didn't find frontmatter — body is verbatim. Tell the
124			// user so they don't think Ctrl+I silently no-op'd.
125			m.err = "找不到 frontmatter（請貼上開頭含 --- ... --- 的 markdown）"
126			return
127		}
128		if parsed.Title != "" {
129			m.title.SetValue(parsed.Title)
130		}
131		m.body.SetValue(parsed.Body)
132		m.err = ""
133	}
134	
135	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
136		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
137		body := m.body.Value()
138		if title == "" {
139			m.err = "title is required"
140			return m, nil
141		}
142		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
143			m.err = "body is required"
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		ctx := context.Background()
147		u := m.deps.User
148		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
149		if err != nil {
150			m.err = err.Error()
151			return m, nil
152		}
153		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
154			m.err = err.Error()
155			return m, nil
156		}
157		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
158		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
159		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
160			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
161				BoardID:      m.boardID,
162				ArticleID:    art.ID,
163				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
164				Title:        art.Title,
165			})
166		}
167		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
168	}
169	
170	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
171		var b strings.Builder
172		b.WriteString("\n")
173		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
174		b.WriteString("\n\n")
175	
176		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
177		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
178	
179		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
180		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
181	
182		if m.err != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
184		}
185		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Ctrl+I import markdown · Esc cancel"))
186		b.WriteString("\n")
187		return b.String()
188	}
189	
190	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
191		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
192		for i, ln := range lines {
193			if i == 0 {
194				continue
195			}
196			lines[i] = prefix + ln
197		}
198		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
199	}

 `./internal/tui/runewidth.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"regexp"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode/utf8"
7	
8		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
9	)
10	
11	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
12	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
13	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
14	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
15	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
16		if w <= 0 {
17			return ""
18		}
19		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
20		if cw == w {
21			return s
22		}
23		if cw > w {
24			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
25		}
26		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
27	}
28	
29	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
30	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
31		if w <= 0 {
32			return ""
33		}
34		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
35		if cw == w {
36			return s
37		}
38		if cw > w {
39			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
40		}
41		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
42	}
43	
44	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
45	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
46		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
47			return s
48		}
49		if w <= 1 {
50			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
51		}
52		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
53	}
54	
55	// Width returns the display width of s.
56	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
57	
58	// ansiCSIRe matches a CSI escape sequence: ESC '[' optional-params letter.
59	// SGR (color), cursor moves, and most other terminal control codes used by
60	// banners follow this shape. Other escape kinds (OSC, DCS) are left
61	// untouched — banners almost never carry them.
62	var ansiCSIRe = regexp.MustCompile("\x1b\\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]")
63	
64	// stripCSI returns s with CSI escape sequences removed. Use it for any
65	// width / column calculation that involves banner content (or any other
66	// string that may carry inline color codes). The four helpers above
67	// intentionally do NOT call this — they're the article-list table path
68	// where input is plain text, and stripping every line would just be
69	// overhead.
70	func stripCSI(s string) string {
71		return ansiCSIRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
72	}
73	
74	// bannerVisualWidth returns the display width of s with CSI escapes
75	// excluded from the count.
76	func bannerVisualWidth(s string) int {
77		return runewidth.StringWidth(stripCSI(s))
78	}
79	
80	// bannerTruncate clips s to display-width w, preserving inline CSI runs
81	// (color codes are zero-width). On overflow the result ends with "…"
82	// (which counts toward the budget). If the input contained any CSI, the
83	// result is suffixed with `\x1b[0m` so a forgotten reset doesn't bleed
84	// into subsequent terminal output.
85	func bannerTruncate(s string, w int) string {
86		if w <= 0 {
87			return ""
88		}
89		if bannerVisualWidth(s) <= w {
90			return ensureANSIReset(s)
91		}
92		budget := w - 1 // reserve one cell for "…"
93		var out strings.Builder
94		visible := 0
95		cursor := 0
96		for _, loc := range ansiCSIRe.FindAllStringIndex(s, -1) {
97			if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:loc[0]], &visible, budget) {
98				out.WriteString("…")
99				return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
100			}
101			out.WriteString(s[loc[0]:loc[1]])
102			cursor = loc[1]
103		}
104		if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:], &visible, budget) {
105			out.WriteString("…")
106			return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
107		}
108		out.WriteString("…")
109		return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
110	}
111	
112	func writeBodyUpToBudget(out *strings.Builder, body string, visible *int, budget int) bool {
113		for i := 0; i < len(body); {
114			r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(body[i:])
115			rw := runewidth.RuneWidth(r)
116			if *visible+rw > budget {
117				return false
118			}
119			out.WriteRune(r)
120			*visible += rw
121			i += size
122		}
123		return true
124	}
125	
126	func ensureANSIReset(s string) string {
127		if strings.Contains(s, "\x1b[") && !strings.HasSuffix(s, "\x1b[0m") {
128			return s + "\x1b[0m"
129		}
130		return s
131	}
132
```
</details></tool-use>

# Plan: board/article search + article sort + help-key polish

## Decisions (recommended)

- **Search activation key:** `/` on both list screens — vim-idiomatic, currently unbound, doesn't collide with existing keys.
- **Boards filter:** **client-side**. Board count is small (3 seeded, expected <100 lifetime). No DB churn; no migration.
- **Articles filter:** **server-side** via a new options struct. Loading 100 then filtering client-side gives empty results when the substring matches an article #101+. LIKE `%q%` won't use an index, but `WHERE board_id = ?` keeps the per-board scan tiny.
- **Sort key:** `s` cycles `Newest ↔ Score`. Pinned still floats to top in both modes.
- **Sort field:** **`recommend_score DESC`** (option A). No new column, no migration. Trade-off documented below.
- **Search-mode UX:** dedicated `searchActive bool` + `bubbles/textinput`. While active, every printable key (including `h`, `j`, `k`, `l`, `[`, `]`, `g`, `G`, `M`, `p`, `b`, `B`, `s`) routes to the textinput — same rule as form screens. Only `enter` (confirm), `esc` (cancel + clear), and arrows / `ctrl+u` / `ctrl+c` remain controls.
- **Help-overlay availability:** `?` already works (root.go:93). Issue is discoverability. Add `? help` to the footer hint on board list, board view, article view, and add `/`, `s` to `screenHelp`.

## Order of work

### 1. Store layer (foundation; everything else depends on it)

**`internal/store/articles.go` — extend ListByBoard**

Introduce an options struct so the API scales beyond the current two needs (filter + sort) without a third overload later:

```
type ArticleSort int
const (
    SortNewestFirst ArticleSort = iota // existing default
    SortByScoreDesc
)

type ListArticlesOpts struct {
    Limit       int
    TitleSearch string      // empty = no filter; LIKE '%q%' COLLATE NOCASE
    Sort        ArticleSort
}

func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoardOpts(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, opts ListArticlesOpts) ([]*Article, error)
```

Keep `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` as a thin shim → `ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, ListArticlesOpts{Limit: limit})`. Existing call sites (boardViewModel.Init, ArticleAddedMsg / ArticlePinChangedMsg / ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg handlers in screen_board_view.go, NeighboursOf is unaffected) keep compiling.

SQL shape:
```sql
SELECT ... FROM articles
WHERE board_id = ?
  [AND title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' COLLATE NOCASE]    -- only when TitleSearch != ""
ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL),
         CASE WHEN ?=1 THEN recommend_score END DESC,   -- when Sort=SortByScoreDesc
         created_at DESC, id DESC
LIMIT ?;
```
- Escape `%`, `_`, `\` in TitleSearch before binding (use `strings.NewReplacer`). LIKE pattern is `'%' + escaped + '%'`.
- Pinned-first stays first in ORDER BY for both sorts (matches existing semantics from articles.go:78 and the `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst` test).
- `created_at DESC, id DESC` is the secondary tiebreaker even in score mode — keeps results deterministic when many articles have the same score (very common for fresh posts at score=0).

**`internal/store/articles_test.go` — extend (file already exists, see articles_test.go:50, 468)**

New tests:
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_TitleSearch` — substring match, case-insensitive, doesn't escape into other boards.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_TitleSearch_EscapesLikeWildcards` — title `"50%"` doesn't match `"5"`.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_SortByScore` — three articles with scores 5, 0, 10 surface in 10/5/0 order.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_SortByScore_PinnedStillFirst` — pinned 0-score article precedes unpinned 10-score article.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_TitleSearchAndSort_Combined` — both filters apply; pinned-first preserved.
- `TestArticles_ListByBoard_BackwardCompatible` — explicit assertion that the old shim returns identical rows for `Limit=N` as the previous behavior.

No changes needed in `internal/store/boards.go` (client-side board filter).

### 2. Board list — client-side filter + footer hint

**`internal/tui/screen_board_list.go`** (currently 108 lines, see screen_board_list.go:13)

Add to `boardListModel`:
```
search       textinput.Model
searchActive bool   // input focused & accepting keys
filter       string // confirmed filter (kept after Enter)
filtered     []*store.Board   // recomputed whenever filter or boards changes
```

State machine:
- Idle (filter empty) — full keymap as today.
- Searching (`/` pressed → `searchActive = true`, textinput.Focus()) — every key except `enter`/`esc`/`ctrl+u`/`ctrl+c`/arrows passes to `textinput.Update`. Each keystroke recomputes `filtered`.
- Confirmed (`enter`) — `searchActive = false`, `filter = strings.TrimSpace(search.Value())`, cursor reset to 0. Filter persists; arrows / `j`/`k`/`h`/`l` work normally over `filtered`.
- Cleared (`esc` while idle but filter non-empty) — clear filter, `filtered = boards`, restore cursor to 0.
- Cleared (`esc` while searching) — drop the in-flight query AND any prior filter; back to idle.

Filter function (CJK-aware substring — Go's `strings.Contains` is byte-safe for UTF-8 substrings; case folding via `strings.EqualFold` per-rune is overkill, simply lowercase ASCII portions via `strings.ToLower` on both sides since board names are typically ASCII; if a board ever has CJK in `Name`/`Title` the lowercase is a no-op and substring-match still works):

```
func boardMatches(b *store.Board, q string) bool {
    if q == "" { return true }
    lq := strings.ToLower(q)
    return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Name), lq) ||
           strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Title), lq) ||
           strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Description), lq)
}
```

View:
- When `searchActive`: render textinput on its own line above the list with prompt `搜尋 / : `. Result count subline `(N matches)`.
- When `filter != "" && !searchActive`: render dim header `[搜尋: foo · N results · / 修改 · esc 清除]` above the list.
- Footer hint always ends with `… · / search · ? help · Ctrl+C disconnect`.

Cursor invalidation:
- After every filter recompute: clamp `m.cursor` to `len(filtered)-1` and to 0 if empty. Don't try to anchor by board ID — the user is actively typing, so jumpiness from anchoring would be worse than reset.

**`internal/tui/screen_board_list_test.go`** (extend; current at screen_board_list_test.go:1)

New tests:
- `TestBoardList_SlashEntersSearchMode` — typing `/` then `Te` then `enter` filters to `Test`, cursor at 0.
- `TestBoardList_SearchPreventsHJKLNav` — while searchActive, `h` types into the input, does NOT navigate back. Same for `j`/`k`/`l`/`Q`.
- `TestBoardList_EscClearsFilter` — after a confirmed filter, esc reverts to full list.
- `TestBoardList_FilterCursorClamps` — cursor=2 then filter narrows to 1 result → cursor=0.
- `TestBoardList_FilterByDescription` — query matches `Description` substring.
- `TestBoardList_FilterCJK` — query containing 「閒」 matches `ChitChat` board (whose title is `閒聊`).

### 3. Board view — server-side filter + sort toggle + footer hint

**`internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`** (currently 335 lines, see screen_board_view.go:13)

Add to `boardViewModel`:
```
search       textinput.Model
searchActive bool
filter       string                  // confirmed title filter
sort         store.ArticleSort       // SortNewestFirst (default) or SortByScoreDesc
```

Refactor the four current `ListByBoard` calls (lines 29, 43, 57, 73) into one helper:
```
func (m *boardViewModel) reload(anchorID int64) {
    arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoardOpts(ctx, m.board.ID,
        store.ListArticlesOpts{Limit: 100, TitleSearch: m.filter, Sort: m.sort})
    if err == nil { m.articles = arts }
    if anchorID > 0 { m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID) }
    if m.cursor >= len(arts) { m.cursor = max(0, len(arts)-1) }
}
```

State machine for `/` mirrors board list. `s` cycles sort (only when not searchActive); reload after each toggle, anchor by current article ID so the highlight follows the post.

Key gating during searchActive: every alphanumeric key — including `s`, `M`, `p`, `b`, `B`, `g`, `G`, `j`, `k`, `h`, `l`, `[`, `]` — must NOT trigger their action. Only `enter` confirms, `esc` cancels, `up`/`down`/`PgUp`/`PgDn` still scroll (handy: glance at filtered results while typing).

View additions:
- Search input row when `searchActive` (above the article table).
- `[排序: 推文量↓]` indicator next to the header when `sort != SortNewestFirst`. Use 推文量 in the indicator since that's the user's wording, even though the field is `recommend_score` (note the trade-off in a code comment).
- Footer hint adds `· / search · s sort · ? help`.

Concurrent-broadcast race:
- `ArticleAddedMsg` arriving for this board while a filter is active should respect the filter (just call `reload(anchorID)` — the new helper already passes m.filter). If the new article doesn't match the filter, it just won't appear, which is fine. If the user is typing (`searchActive`), defer the reload until they confirm? — Recommend: still reload silently (the textinput's own ticker won't be disturbed; only `m.articles` changes). The cursor will get re-anchored.

**`internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go`** (extend; current at screen_board_view_test.go:1)

New tests:
- `TestBoardView_SlashEntersSearchMode` + `TestBoardView_SearchPreventsActionKeys` (s, M, p, b, B all type into the input).
- `TestBoardView_FilterByTitle` — seed five articles with mixed titles, type `irst` + enter, expect only the `first` row.
- `TestBoardView_SortToggle_ByScore` — seed three articles, push them to scores 0/3/-1, press `s`, expect order `3, 0, -1` (skipping pinned).
- `TestBoardView_SortToggle_PinnedStillFirst` — pin a 0-score article, ensure it's first even with score sort active.
- `TestBoardView_SortPersistsAcrossReload` — press `s`, then receive `ArticleAddedMsg`, confirm the new ordering is preserved.
- `TestBoardView_FilterAndSort_Combined`.
- `TestBoardView_FilterCursorReanchorsByID` — after filter narrows results, the cursor stays on the same article ID when possible.

### 4. Footer hint + help.go updates

**`internal/tui/screen_board_list.go:104`** — append `· / search · ? help` to the help-line.

**`internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:244, 246`** — append `· / search · s sort · ? help` (and the variant for guest at 246).

**`internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:634`** — append `· ? help`. (No new keys, just the discoverability fix.)

**`internal/tui/help.go:43-49`** (ScreenBoardList block) — add:
```
{"/", "search boards (Esc clears, Enter confirms)"},
{"Esc (filtered)", "clear filter"},
```

**`internal/tui/help.go:51-63`** (ScreenBoardView block) — add:
```
{"/", "search articles by title"},
{"s", "toggle sort (Newest ↔ 推文量)"},
{"Esc (filtered)", "clear filter"},
```

`screenHelp[ScreenArticleView]` already lists every key; no schema change needed there. Globals at help.go:173 already advertise `?`.

**`internal/tui/help_test.go`** — extend whatever parity table tests already exist (see help_test.go) to assert the new entries are reachable on those screens.

## Trade-offs / alternatives considered

- **Why not a `push_count` column?** Cleaner semantics for "推文量". Costs: schema migration (0009-something), `pushes.go` mutation now has to update a counter, an existing test must be re-baselined. The cached `recommend_score` column already exists and does the job. If a future product decision says "score and push-count differ — score subtracts 噓 but 推文量 should count noise", we add the column then. Logged in code as a `// TODO(score-vs-pushcount)` near the new sort branch.
- **Server vs. client filter for articles:** server. The current 100-row cap means client filter would sometimes show 0 hits despite matches existing. SQLite + `WHERE board_id = ?` keeps the LIKE scan O(per-board article count) which is bounded.
- **Why `/` and not `f`?** `f` is not currently used either, but `/` is the universal vim convention and matches user mental model. `f` could be added as an alias.
- **Footer-only indicator vs. modal banner:** footer keeps the article count stable, which matters because list height is part of the layout (banner block at top, articles in middle, hint at bottom). A floating banner would shift everything.
- **Help-key perception fix without keymap change:** the user's complaint is real even though the keybinding works — they just couldn't see `?` on the screens they tried (board list, board view, article view). Pure footer-text fix.

## Risks

- **Cursor invalidation:** filter narrowing will leave `m.cursor` past the end of the slice if not clamped. Both screens need explicit `m.cursor = clamp(m.cursor, 0, len(filtered)-1)` after every reload/filter.
- **Concurrent broadcast during search:** `ArticleAddedMsg` arrives while user is typing. Recommended: still reload (silently honoring m.filter); searchActive textinput is unaffected. Document this in a code comment alongside the helper.
- **CJK width in textinput:** `bubbles/textinput` itself handles CJK reasonably, but `m.title.Width` (post-compose pattern, screen_post_compose.go:62) sets a width budget — apply the same trick: `m.search.Width = max(20, m.width - 12)` at WindowSizeMsg and on first render.
- **`strings.ToLower` for CJK in board filter:** no-op for CJK, fine for ASCII. If we ever add accented Latin board names, switch to `strings.EqualFold` over normalized runes — out of scope now.
- **`enter` overload:** in board list/view, `enter` currently means "open cursored item". When `searchActive`, `enter` must mean "confirm filter". The state machine handles this — cleanly gated by `searchActive`.
- **Backward-compat of ListByBoard shim:** must remain identical in the limit-only case so the existing `TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst`, `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst`, `TestArticles_ListByBoard_Limit` tests pass without modification.
- **Pinned-first interaction with score sort:** if user pins a -10 score article, it stays at top in score mode. This is the right call (pin is mod-explicit) but worth a code comment explaining the override.

```text
1	# Admin/Mod: Lock Comments / Arrows-Only on Articles
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	PTT-style boards routinely have 板規 / 公告 articles where the BM wants to **stop discussion** entirely, or **prevent 推/噓 voting** while still allowing neutral 箭頭 follow-ups. Today the BBS has no per-article moderation control over comments — anyone non-guest can push/boo/arrow on anything.
6	
7	This plan adds a single article-level `comments_mode` state, settable by mod+, that gates the `+`/`-`/`=` push flow and is broadcast live to every session viewing the article or its board. It mirrors the existing `pinned_at` moderation pattern (mod-only, not author-admitted) so the surface stays internally consistent.
8	
9	## Decisions (locked with the user)
10	
11	- **Schema shape**: single 3-way enum `comments_mode ∈ {open, arrows_only, locked}`. Mutually exclusive; `locked` strictly stronger than `arrows_only`.
12	- **Permission**: `Role.AtLeast(RoleMod)` only. Author of the article cannot self-lock — mirrors `ArticleRepo.SetPinned` (`internal/store/articles.go:180`), NOT `Update`/`Delete`.
13	- **Display scope**: badge in both the article list (`screen_board_view.go`) and the article view header (`screen_article_view.go`).
14	- **Live broadcast**: yes — new `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg{BoardID, ArticleID, Mode}` mirroring `ArticlePinChangedMsg`; receivers re-fetch from DB (DB is source of truth).
15	- **Defense in depth**: gate three layers — UI early (`openPush`), UI late (`updatePushInput` Enter), and store (`PushRepo.Create` inside the existing tx). The store gate is non-negotiable because multiple sessions can race against a mode change.
16	
17	## State semantics
18	
19	| Mode          | `+` (push) | `-` (boo) | `=` (arrow) |
20	|---------------|:---------:|:---------:|:-----------:|
21	| `open`        | ✅        | ✅        | ✅          |
22	| `arrows_only` | ❌        | ❌        | ✅          |
23	| `locked`      | ❌        | ❌        | ❌          |
24	
25	Existing 推/噓 pushes on an article are **not** retroactively deleted when mode changes — only new pushes are blocked. `recommend_score` is therefore preserved across mode flips. Push **deletion** by author/mod is unaffected by mode (mods can always clean up).
26

28	
29	- **`internal/store/migrations/0008_articles_comments_mode.sql`** — `ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN comments_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open' CHECK (comments_mode IN ('open','arrows_only','locked'))`. No index — column is only read inline with article rows. Follows existing `0007_articles_pinned_at.sql` style.
30

```md
# Search & Sort: Boards, Articles, plus Help-Key Polish

## Context

Two complementary discoverability gaps surfaced from real usage:

1. **No way to find content quickly.** The board list grows past what fits on
   one page; the article list within a board paginates a hundred at a time
   without any way to narrow by title. Sorting is locked to "newest-first
   (with pinned floating to top)" — there's no way to surface popular threads.
2. **`?` help feels broken.** The help system at `internal/tui/help.go` is
   actually fully wired (`root.go:81-101` intercepts `?` globally and toggles
   `m.helpVisible`), but the **footer hint lines** on Board List, Board View,
   and Article View don't advertise `? help`, while the Main Menu does. Users
   who try `?` on form screens — where it's intentionally suppressed by
   `helpAvailable()` so the character types literally — also conclude the
   feature is broken. The keybinding works; the surface area lies.

This plan adds vim-idiomatic `/` search on both list screens, an `s` sort
toggle in the board view, and fixes the footer-hint inconsistency in the
same change set.

## Decisions (locked with the user)

- **Search activation key:** `/` on both list screens (vim convention,
  currently unbound, doesn't collide).
- **Boards filter:** **client-side**. Boards are bounded (~3 seeded today,
  expected lifetime <100). No DB churn, no migration.
- **Articles filter:** **server-side**. Extend `ArticleRepo.ListByBoard`
  with an options struct; add `WHERE title LIKE ? COLLATE NOCASE` when a
  query is set. Loading 100 then filtering client-side would silently miss
  matches in older articles; that surprise is worse than the cost of an
  un-indexed LIKE bounded to one board.
- **Sort field:** **`recommend_score DESC`**. The cached
  `articles.recommend_score` already exists (signed sum of +1/-1/0 from
  pushes) — semantically "popular/hot", which matches the user's intent
  for 推文量 排序. No `push_count` column, no migration.
- **Sort key:** `s` cycles `Newest ↔ 推文量(score)`. Pinned articles still
  float to top in both modes (matches existing
  `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst`).
- **Help-key polish:** add `? help · / search` to Board List footer,
  `? help · / search · s sort` to Board View footer, `? help` to Article
  View footer. Add the new keys to `screenHelp` in `help.go`. Form screens
  unchanged (still suppressed by `helpAvailable()`).

## State semantics — search mode

Both list screens share the same four-state machine:

| State          | Trigger                          | Behaviour                                                                                   |
|----------------|----------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **idle**       | initial; or `Esc` from filtered  | full list visible, all keymap normal                                                        |
| **searching**  | `/` pressed                      | textinput focused; printable keys (incl. `h`/`j`/`k`/`l`/`s`/`p`/`M`/`b`/`B`) type into it  |
| **confirmed**  | `Enter` from searching           | input dismissed; filter persists; cursor reset to 0; `[搜尋: foo · N]` indicator shown      |
| **cleared**    | `Esc` from searching or confirmed| filter wiped; back to idle                                                                  |

Critically: while **searching**, vim navigation is suspended — same rule
as form screens (CLAUDE.md: form screens skip `h`/`l` so text input
works). Only `Enter`, `Esc`, arrows/PgUp/PgDn (for glancing at filtered
results while typing), `Ctrl+U`, `Ctrl+C` remain as controls.

## Files to add

None. All work fits in existing files; no new migrations.

## Files to modify

### `internal/store/articles.go`

Refactor list path to take an options struct so future sort/filter
extensions don't keep growing positional args.

- Add types near the top (after the `Article` struct, before
  `articleColumns`):
  \```go
  type ArticleSort int
  const (
      SortNewestFirst ArticleSort = iota // existing default
      SortByScoreDesc
  )

  type ListArticlesOpts struct {
      Limit       int          // 0 → no limit; callers pass 100 today
      TitleSearch string       // empty → no filter; LIKE '%q%' COLLATE NOCASE
      Sort        ArticleSort
  }
  \```
- New method `ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, opts)` appended after the
  current `ListByBoard` (`articles.go:69-94`). SQL shape:
  \```sql
  SELECT <articleColumns>
  FROM articles
  WHERE board_id = ?
    [AND title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\' COLLATE NOCASE]   -- only when TitleSearch != ""
  ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL),
           CASE WHEN @sortByScore THEN recommend_score END DESC,
           created_at DESC, id DESC
  LIMIT ?;
  \```
  - Escape `%`, `_`, `\` in `TitleSearch` via
    `strings.NewReplacer(`\`, `\\`, `%`, `\%`, `_`, `\_`)` then wrap as
    `'%' + escaped + '%'` before binding.
  - Built dynamically: branch on whether `TitleSearch != ""` (adds the
    AND clause + bind) and whether `Sort == SortByScoreDesc` (adds the
    `recommend_score DESC` ordering layer). The `pinned-first` and the
    `created_at DESC, id DESC` tiebreaker stay constant in both modes.
  - `created_at DESC, id DESC` as the tail tiebreaker is essential in
    score mode — many fresh posts share `score=0` and need a stable
    secondary ordering.
- Existing `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` becomes a thin shim:
  \```go
  return r.ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, ListArticlesOpts{Limit: limit})
  \```
  All current call sites in `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go` (29, 43,
  57, 73 per exploration) keep compiling unchanged. Existing tests
  (`TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst`, `..._Limit`, `..._PinnedFirst`)
  remain unmodified — that's the regression check on the shim.

### `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`

- Add to `boardViewModel` struct (currently lines 13-21):
  \```go
  search       textinput.Model

- New private helper `reload(anchorID int64)` replaces the four
  ad-hoc `Articles().ListByBoard(...)` call sites (lines 29, 43, 57,
  73):
  \```go
  func (m *boardViewModel) reload(anchorID int64) {
      arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoardOpts(ctx, m.board.ID,
          store.ListArticlesOpts{Limit: 100, TitleSearch: m.filter, Sort: m.sort})
      if err != nil { m.loadErr = err; return }
      m.articles = arts
      if anchorID > 0 { m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID) }
      if m.cursor >= len(arts) { m.cursor = max(0, len(arts)-1) }
  }
  \```
  Reuse the existing `findCursorByID` helper (lines 52-77) — it already
  handles the missing-anchor (cursor=0) case. **Concurrent broadcast
  semantics:** when `ArticleAddedMsg`/`ArticlePinChangedMsg`/
  `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg` arrive while `searchActive`, still
  call `reload` silently — the textinput's own state is not disturbed
  because we only mutate `m.articles`/`m.cursor`. This matches existing
  message-driven re-fetch semantics ("DB is source of truth" — CLAUDE.md).
- Update key handling around the existing switch (lines 79-152):
  - `case "/"` (only when not searchActive): set `searchActive = true`,

  - When `searchActive`: route `enter` to confirm

  - When idle and `m.filter != ""`: `esc` clears the filter and reloads.
  - `case "s"` (only when not searchActive): cycle
    `m.sort = (m.sort + 1) % 2`, then `reload(currentAnchorID)`.
- View() updates (lines 157-252):
  - When `searchActive`: render `m.search.View()` on its own line above
    the article table with prompt `搜尋 / 標題: `.
  - When `!searchActive && m.filter != ""`: render dim header
    `[搜尋: <q> · <N> 筆 · / 修改 · esc 清除]` above the table.
  - When `m.sort == SortByScoreDesc`: render `[排序: 推文量↓]` next to
    the column header (use 推文量 in the user-facing label per the
    user's wording, even though the underlying field is
    `recommend_score` — leave a one-line code comment near the sort
    branch noting the score-vs-pushcount trade-off).
  - Footer hint (lines 244 / 246 — main + guest variants): append
    `· / search · s sort · ? help`.
- Initialize textinput in `newBoardViewModel`: width budget mirrors the
  post-compose pattern (`screen_post_compose.go:62`):
  `m.search.Width = max(20, m.width - 12)`, also set on `WindowSizeMsg`.

### `internal/tui/screen_board_list.go`

Same pattern as board view, with client-side filter so no store change.

- Add to `boardListModel` (lines 13-20):
  \```go
  search       textinput.Model
  searchActive bool
  filter       string
  filtered     []*store.Board   // recomputed from m.boards whenever filter changes
  \```
- New `recomputeFilter()` method:
  \```go
  func (m *boardListModel) recomputeFilter() {
      if m.filter == "" {
          m.filtered = m.boards
      } else {
          lq := strings.ToLower(m.filter)
          m.filtered = m.filtered[:0]
          for _, b := range m.boards {
              if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Name), lq) ||
                 strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Title), lq) ||
                 strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Description), lq) {
                  m.filtered = append(m.filtered, b)
              }
          }
      }
      if m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
          m.cursor = max(0, len(m.filtered)-1)
      }
  }
  \```
  Note: `strings.ToLower` is a no-op for CJK runes, which is fine —
  CJK matching falls back to direct rune-substring comparison and still
  works. If accented Latin board names ever appear, switch to a folded
  comparison; out of scope today.
- Key handling (lines 29-62) gains the same `/` / `enter` / `esc`
  state-machine branch as board view. All cursor/render logic switches
  from `m.boards` to `m.filtered`.
- View() (lines 65-107):
  - Search input row when `searchActive`.
  - Indicator line when filter is active but not searching.
  - Footer (line 104): append `· / search · ? help`.

## Order of work

1. **Store layer first**: `articles.go` (`ListByBoardOpts` + shim) + new
   tests. Run `go test -race ./internal/store/...` — gates everything
   downstream.
2. **Board list** (simpler, no store dependency): textinput field,
   state machine, `recomputeFilter`, view, footer. Tests.
3. **Board view**: refactor four `ListByBoard` calls to `reload(...)`;
   add filter + sort UI. Tests.
4. **Footer hints + `help.go`**: trivial textual edits across three
   files; no test changes beyond the optional `help_test.go` extension.

## Verification

\```bash
make test-race                                      # full suite
go test -race -run 'ListByBoardOpts|BoardList_|BoardView_' \
    ./internal/store/... ./internal/tui/...         # focused

# manual two-user smoke (CLAUDE.md "manual two-user smoke")
make hostkey                                        # if not already done
make run &                                          # boots :2222
ssh alice@localhost -p 2222
# in TUI:
#   board list → press / → type "test" → Enter, see narrowed list
#   open a board → press / → type a known title fragment → Enter
#   press s → list reorders by 推文量
#   press ? → help overlay shows / and s entries
#   press Esc on filtered list → filter clears
#   press / on register form (sshing as `new`) → ? must NOT trigger
#     help overlay (form screen suppression preserved)
\```

Specifically watch for:
- Cursor position survives filter narrow/widen without panicking.
- Live `ArticleAddedMsg` while a filter or non-default sort is active
  doesn't blow away the user's selection or force-reset the sort.
- Footer hints render `? help` on the three target screens; nothing
  changes on form screens (compose, register, password change, banner
  edit).

## Risks & trade-offs

- **`recommend_score` ≠ literal push count.** A future product
  decision may want "raw push amount excluding 噓" — that's a
  schema-level change (add `push_count`, maintain in PushRepo).
  Logged as a `// TODO(score-vs-pushcount)` near the score-sort branch.
- **Un-indexed LIKE.** `WHERE title LIKE '%q%'` is a sequential scan
  bounded to one board. At BBS scale (hundreds-to-low-thousands of
  articles per active board) this is fine; for an indexed solution we'd
  need FTS5 (currently not compiled into the pure-Go
  `modernc.org/sqlite` build) or a prefix index. Out of scope.
- **Cursor invalidation.** Both screens must clamp `m.cursor` after
  every filter recompute or reload. Tests guard this.
- **`enter` overload.** In idle state, `enter` opens the cursored item;
  in `searching` state, `enter` confirms the filter. Cleanly gated by
  `searchActive`.
- **Pinned overrides score sort.** A mod-pinned -10 article still floats
  to top in score mode. Intentional — pin is an explicit moderation
  signal stronger than score. Document with a code comment.
- **Backward-compat shim.** `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` must
  produce byte-identical output to the legacy implementation when
  called with the same args; the existing
  `TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst|Limit|PinnedFirst` tests are
  the regression guard and must not be modified.
- **CJK width in textinput.** `bubbles/textinput` handles CJK width
  correctly, but the Width field needs to be set on `WindowSizeMsg`
  (mirrors `screen_post_compose.go:62`). Otherwise long queries wrap
  oddly.
- **Help-key on form screens stays suppressed.** Search mode is *not*
  a form screen at the `helpAvailable()` level, but the `searchActive`
  state intercepts `?` (because all printable keys route to the input)
  — which is correct. Users typing `?` into a search query mean it
  literally.

## Approved Plan:
# Search & Sort: Boards, Articles, plus Help-Key Polish

## Context

Two complementary discoverability gaps surfaced from real usage:

1. **No way to find content quickly.** The board list grows past what fits on
   one page; the article list within a board paginates a hundred at a time
   without any way to narrow by title. Sorting is locked to "newest-first
   (with pinned floating to top)" — there's no way to surface popular threads.
2. **`?` help feels broken.** The help system at `internal/tui/help.go` is
   actually fully wired (`root.go:81-101` intercepts `?` globally and toggles
   `m.helpVisible`), but the **footer hint lines** on Board List, Board View,
   and Article View don't advertise `? help`, while the Main Menu does. Users
   who try `?` on form screens — where it's intentionally suppressed by
   `helpAvailable()` so the character types literally — also conclude the
   feature is broken. The keybinding works; the surface area lies.

This plan adds vim-idiomatic `/` search on both list screens, an `s` sort
toggle in the board view, and fixes the footer-hint inconsistency in the
same change set.

## Decisions (locked with the user)

- **Search activation key:** `/` on both list screens (vim convention,
  currently unbound, doesn't collide).
- **Boards filter:** **client-side**. Boards are bounded (~3 seeded today,
  expected lifetime <100). No DB churn, no migration.
- **Articles filter:** **server-side**. Extend `ArticleRepo.ListByBoard`
  with an options struct; add `WHERE title LIKE ? COLLATE NOCASE` when a
  query is set. Loading 100 then filtering client-side would silently miss
  matches in older articles; that surprise is worse than the cost of an
  un-indexed LIKE bounded to one board.
- **Sort field:** **`recommend_score DESC`**. The cached
  `articles.recommend_score` already exists (signed sum of +1/-1/0 from
  pushes) — semantically "popular/hot", which matches the user's intent
  for 推文量 排序. No `push_count` column, no migration.
- **Sort key:** `s` cycles `Newest ↔ 推文量(score)`. Pinned articles still
  float to top in both modes (matches existing
  `TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst`).
- **Help-key polish:** add `? help · / search` to Board List footer,
  `? help · / search · s sort` to Board View footer, `? help` to Article
  View footer. Add the new keys to `screenHelp` in `help.go`. Form screens
  unchanged (still suppressed by `helpAvailable()`).

## State semantics — search mode

Both list screens share the same four-state machine:

| State          | Trigger                          | Behaviour                                                                                   |
|----------------|----------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **idle**       | initial; or `Esc` from filtered  | full list visible, all keymap normal                                                        |
| **searching**  | `/` pressed                      | textinput focused; printable keys (incl. `h`/`j`/`k`/`l`/`s`/`p`/`M`/`b`/`B`) type into it  |
| **confirmed**  | `Enter` from searching           | input dismissed; filter persists; cursor reset to 0; `[搜尋: foo · N]` indicator shown      |
| **cleared**    | `Esc` from searching or confirmed| filter wiped; back to idle                                                                  |

Critically: while **searching**, vim navigation is suspended — same rule
as form screens (CLAUDE.md: form screens skip `h`/`l` so text input
works). Only `Enter`, `Esc`, arrows/PgUp/PgDn (for glancing at filtered
results while typing), `Ctrl+U`, `Ctrl+C` remain as controls.

## Files to add

None. All work fits in existing files; no new migrations.

## Files to modify

### `internal/store/articles.go`

Refactor list path to take an options struct so future sort/filter
extensions don't keep growing positional args.

- Add types near the top (after the `Article` struct, before
  `articleColumns`):
  ```go
  type ArticleSort int
  const (
      SortNewestFirst ArticleSort = iota // existing default
      SortByScoreDesc
  )

  type ListArticlesOpts struct {
      Limit       int          // 0 → no limit; callers pass 100 today
      TitleSearch string       // empty → no filter; LIKE '%q%' COLLATE NOCASE
      Sort        ArticleSort
  }
  ```
- New method `ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, opts)` appended after the
  current `ListByBoard` (`articles.go:69-94`). SQL shape:
  ```sql
  SELECT <articleColumns>
  FROM articles
  WHERE board_id = ?
    [AND title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\' COLLATE NOCASE]   -- only when TitleSearch != ""
  ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL),
           CASE WHEN @sortByScore THEN recommend_score END DESC,
           created_at DESC, id DESC
  LIMIT ?;
  ```
  - Escape `%`, `_`, `\` in `TitleSearch` via
    `strings.NewReplacer(`\`, `\\`, `%`, `\%`, `_`, `\_`)` then wrap as
    `'%' + escaped + '%'` before binding.
  - Built dynamically: branch on whether `TitleSearch != ""` (adds the
    AND clause + bind) and whether `Sort == SortByScoreDesc` (adds the
    `recommend_score DESC` ordering layer). The `pinned-first` and the
    `created_at DESC, id DESC` tiebreaker stay constant in both modes.
  - `created_at DESC, id DESC` as the tail tiebreaker is essential in
    score mode — many fresh posts share `score=0` and need a stable
    secondary ordering.
- Existing `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` becomes a thin shim:
  ```go
  return r.ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, ListArticlesOpts{Limit: limit})
  ```
  All current call sites in `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go` (29, 43,
  57, 73 per exploration) keep compiling unchanged. Existing tests
  (`TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst`, `..._Limit`, `..._PinnedFirst`)
  remain unmodified — that's the regression check on the shim.

### `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`

- Add to `boardViewModel` struct (currently lines 13-21):
  ```go
  search       textinput.Model
  searchActive bool             // input focused & accepting keys
  filter       string            // confirmed title filter
  sort         store.ArticleSort // SortNewestFirst (default) or SortByScoreDesc
  ```
- New private helper `reload(anchorID int64)` replaces the four
  ad-hoc `Articles().ListByBoard(...)` call sites (lines 29, 43, 57,
  73):
  ```go
  func (m *boardViewModel) reload(anchorID int64) {
      arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoardOpts(ctx, m.board.ID,
          store.ListArticlesOpts{Limit: 100, TitleSearch: m.filter, Sort: m.sort})
      if err != nil { m.loadErr = err; return }
      m.articles = arts
      if anchorID > 0 { m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID) }
      if m.cursor >= len(arts) { m.cursor = max(0, len(arts)-1) }
  }
  ```
  Reuse the existing `findCursorByID` helper (lines 52-77) — it already
  handles the missing-anchor (cursor=0) case. **Concurrent broadcast
  semantics:** when `ArticleAddedMsg`/`ArticlePinChangedMsg`/
  `ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg` arrive while `searchActive`, still
  call `reload` silently — the textinput's own state is not disturbed
  because we only mutate `m.articles`/`m.cursor`. This matches existing
  message-driven re-fetch semantics ("DB is source of truth" — CLAUDE.md).
- Update key handling around the existing switch (lines 79-152):
  - `case "/"` (only when not searchActive): set `searchActive = true`,
    `m.search.Focus()`, return `m.search.Cursor.BlinkCmd()`.
  - When `searchActive`: route `enter` to confirm
    (`m.filter = strings.TrimSpace(m.search.Value())`,
    `searchActive = false`, `m.search.Blur()`, `m.cursor = 0`,
    `reload(0)`); route `esc` to cancel (drop in-flight value AND prior
    filter — `m.search.SetValue("")`, `m.filter = ""`, `searchActive =
    false`, `reload(0)`); route every other key (incl. `h`/`l`/`s`/`M`/
    `p`/`b`/`B`/`g`/`G`/letters/punct) through `m.search.Update(msg)`,
    then call `filterPreview()` if you want live preview (optional —
    cheap-and-correct version: only filter on Enter).
  - When idle and `m.filter != ""`: `esc` clears the filter and reloads.
  - `case "s"` (only when not searchActive): cycle
    `m.sort = (m.sort + 1) % 2`, then `reload(currentAnchorID)`.
- View() updates (lines 157-252):
  - When `searchActive`: render `m.search.View()` on its own line above
    the article table with prompt `搜尋 / 標題: `.
  - When `!searchActive && m.filter != ""`: render dim header
    `[搜尋: <q> · <N> 筆 · / 修改 · esc 清除]` above the table.
  - When `m.sort == SortByScoreDesc`: render `[排序: 推文量↓]` next to
    the column header (use 推文量 in the user-facing label per the
    user's wording, even though the underlying field is
    `recommend_score` — leave a one-line code comment near the sort
    branch noting the score-vs-pushcount trade-off).
  - Footer hint (lines 244 / 246 — main + guest variants): append
    `· / search · s sort · ? help`.
- Initialize textinput in `newBoardViewModel`: width budget mirrors the
  post-compose pattern (`screen_post_compose.go:62`):
  `m.search.Width = max(20, m.width - 12)`, also set on `WindowSizeMsg`.

### `internal/tui/screen_board_list.go`

Same pattern as board view, with client-side filter so no store change.

- Add to `boardListModel` (lines 13-20):
  ```go
  search       textinput.Model
  searchActive bool
  filter       string
  filtered     []*store.Board   // recomputed from m.boards whenever filter changes
  ```
- New `recomputeFilter()` method:
  ```go
  func (m *boardListModel) recomputeFilter() {
      if m.filter == "" {
          m.filtered = m.boards
      } else {
          lq := strings.ToLower(m.filter)
          m.filtered = m.filtered[:0]
          for _, b := range m.boards {
              if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Name), lq) ||
                 strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Title), lq) ||
                 strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Description), lq) {
                  m.filtered = append(m.filtered, b)
              }
          }
      }
      if m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
          m.cursor = max(0, len(m.filtered)-1)
      }
  }
  ```
  Note: `strings.ToLower` is a no-op for CJK runes, which is fine —
  CJK matching falls back to direct rune-substring comparison and still
  works. If accented Latin board names ever appear, switch to a folded
  comparison; out of scope today.
- Key handling (lines 29-62) gains the same `/` / `enter` / `esc`
  state-machine branch as board view. All cursor/render logic switches
  from `m.boards` to `m.filtered`.
- View() (lines 65-107):
  - Search input row when `searchActive`.
  - Indicator line when filter is active but not searching.
  - Footer (line 104): append `· / search · ? help`.

  Query with `TitleSearch: "first"` → expect 2 hits (case-insensitive),
  newest-first.

- `TestArticles_ListByBoardOpts_SortByScore` — seed three articles,

## Order of work

1. **Store layer first**: `articles.go` (`ListByBoardOpts` + shim) + new
   tests. Run `go test -race ./internal/store/...` — gates everything
   downstream.
2. **Board list** (simpler, no store dependency): textinput field,
   state machine, `recomputeFilter`, view, footer. Tests.
3. **Board view**: refactor four `ListByBoard` calls to `reload(...)`;
   add filter + sort UI. Tests.
4. **Footer hints + `help.go`**: trivial textual edits across three
   files; no test changes beyond the optional `help_test.go` extension.

## Verification

```bash
make test-race                                      # full suite
go test -race -run 'ListByBoardOpts|BoardList_|BoardView_' \
    ./internal/store/... ./internal/tui/...         # focused

# manual two-user smoke (CLAUDE.md "manual two-user smoke")
make hostkey                                        # if not already done
make run &                                          # boots :2222
ssh alice@localhost -p 2222
# in TUI:
#   board list → press / → type "test" → Enter, see narrowed list
#   open a board → press / → type a known title fragment → Enter
#   press s → list reorders by 推文量
#   press ? → help overlay shows / and s entries
#   press Esc on filtered list → filter clears
#   press / on register form (sshing as `new`) → ? must NOT trigger
#     help overlay (form screen suppression preserved)
```

Specifically watch for:
- Cursor position survives filter narrow/widen without panicking.
- Live `ArticleAddedMsg` while a filter or non-default sort is active
  doesn't blow away the user's selection or force-reset the sort.
- Footer hints render `? help` on the three target screens; nothing
  changes on form screens (compose, register, password change, banner
  edit).

## Risks & trade-offs

- **`recommend_score` ≠ literal push count.** A future product
  decision may want "raw push amount excluding 噓" — that's a
  schema-level change (add `push_count`, maintain in PushRepo).
  Logged as a `// TODO(score-vs-pushcount)` near the score-sort branch.
- **Un-indexed LIKE.** `WHERE title LIKE '%q%'` is a sequential scan
  bounded to one board. At BBS scale (hundreds-to-low-thousands of
  articles per active board) this is fine; for an indexed solution we'd
  need FTS5 (currently not compiled into the pure-Go
  `modernc.org/sqlite` build) or a prefix index. Out of scope.
- **Cursor invalidation.** Both screens must clamp `m.cursor` after
  every filter recompute or reload. Tests guard this.
- **`enter` overload.** In idle state, `enter` opens the cursored item;
  in `searching` state, `enter` confirms the filter. Cleanly gated by
  `searchActive`.
- **Pinned overrides score sort.** A mod-pinned -10 article still floats
  to top in score mode. Intentional — pin is an explicit moderation
  signal stronger than score. Document with a code comment.
- **Backward-compat shim.** `ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, limit)` must
  produce byte-identical output to the legacy implementation when
  called with the same args; the existing
  `TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst|Limit|PinnedFirst` tests are
  the regression guard and must not be modified.
- **CJK width in textinput.** `bubbles/textinput` handles CJK width
  correctly, but the Width field needs to be set on `WindowSizeMsg`
  (mirrors `screen_post_compose.go:62`). Otherwise long queries wrap
  oddly.
- **Help-key on form screens stays suppressed.** Search mode is *not*
  a form screen at the `helpAvailable()` level, but the `searchActive`
  state intercepts `?` (because all printable keys route to the input)
  — which is correct. Users typing `?` into a search query mean it
  literally.
