```text
55		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at, comments_mode`
56	
57	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
58		a := &Article{}
59		var commentsMode string
60		err := row.Scan(
61			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
62			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
63			&commentsMode,
64		)
65		a.CommentsMode = CommentsMode(commentsMode)
66		return a, err
67	}
68	
69	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
70		if limit <= 0 {
71			limit = 50
72		}
73		// Pinned articles surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1);
74		// within each group, newest first by created_at then id (the latter
75		// disambiguates rows that share a second-resolution timestamp).
76		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
77			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ?
78			 ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
79			boardID, limit,
80		)
81		if err != nil {
82			return nil, err
83		}
84		defer rows.Close()
85		var out []*Article
86		for rows.Next() {
87			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
88			if err != nil {
89				return nil, err
90			}
91			out = append(out, a)
92		}
93		return out, rows.Err()
94	}
95	
96	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
97		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
98		a, err := scanArticle(row)
99		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
100			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
101		}
102		return a, err
103	}
104	
105	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
106	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
107	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
108	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
109	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
110		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
111			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
112			boardID, articleID,
113		)
114		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
115			return 0, 0, scanErr
116		}
117		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
118			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
119			boardID, articleID,
120		)
121		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
122			return 0, 0, scanErr
123		}
124		return prev, next, nil
125	}
126	
127	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
128		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
129		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
130		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
131			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
132			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
133		)
134		if err != nil {
135			return nil, err
136		}
137		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
138		if err != nil {
139			return nil, err
140		}
141		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
142		return scanArticle(row)
143	}
144	
145	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
146	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
147	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
148	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
149	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
150		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
151		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
152		var authorID int64
153		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
154			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
155		).Scan(&authorID)
156		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
157			return ErrArticleNotFound
158		}
159		if err != nil {
160			return err
161		}
162		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
163			return ErrPermissionDenied
164		}
165		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
166		return err
167	}
168	
169	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
170	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
171	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
172	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
173	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
174	// edit flow.
175	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
176		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
177		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
178		var authorID int64
179		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
180			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
181		).Scan(&authorID)
182		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
183			return ErrArticleNotFound
184		}
185		if err != nil {
186			return err
187		}
188		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
189			return ErrPermissionDenied
190		}
191		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
192			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
193			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
194		)
195		return err
196	}
197	
198	// SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission
199	// shape mirrors BoardRepo.UpdateBanner: requesterRole must be at-or-above
200	// mod. Pinning is a moderation action — unlike Update/Delete it does NOT
201	// admit the article author. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row is gone,
202	// ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
203	//
204	// Multi-pin per board is supported: the call simply sets pinned_at; nothing
205	// rejects a second pinned row on the same board.
206	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error {
207		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
208			return ErrPermissionDenied
209		}
210		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
211		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
212		var exists int64
213		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
214			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
215		).Scan(&exists)
216		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
217			return ErrArticleNotFound
218		}
219		if err != nil {
220			return err
221		}
222		if pinned {
223			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
224				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
225			)
226		} else {
227			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
228				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = NULL WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
229			)
230		}
231		return err
232	}
233	
234	// SetCommentsMode flips an article's comments_mode (open / arrows_only /
235	// locked). Permission shape mirrors SetPinned: requesterRole must be
236	// at-or-above mod, and unlike Update/Delete this does NOT admit the
237	// article author — locking comments is a moderation action. Returns
238	// ErrInvalidCommentsMode if mode is not one of the three constants,
239	// ErrPermissionDenied / ErrArticleNotFound otherwise. requesterID is
240	// accepted for signature parity with the other mutators (Pin/Update/Delete)
241	// but unused for the gate.
242	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetCommentsMode(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, mode CommentsMode) error {
243		if !mode.Valid() {
244			return ErrInvalidCommentsMode
245		}
246		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
247			return ErrPermissionDenied
248		}
249		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
250		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
251		var exists int64
252		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
253			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
254		).Scan(&exists)
255		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
256			return ErrArticleNotFound
257		}
258		if err != nil {
259			return err
260		}
261		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
262			`UPDATE articles SET comments_mode = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(mode), articleID,
263		)
264		return err
265	}
266
```

```text
468	func TestArticles_ListByBoard_PinnedFirst(t *testing.T) {
469		ctx := context.Background()
470		st := storetest.New(t)
471		mod := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "mod", "")
472		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
473	
474		// Create four articles in chronological order. Pin the second one
475		// (oldest pin candidate that isn't the very first row, so we can prove
476		// it bubbles to the top regardless of created_at).
477		titles := []string{"first", "second", "third", "fourth"}
478		ids := make(map[string]int64, len(titles))
479		for _, ti := range titles {
480			a, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, mod.ID, mod.UserID, ti, "")
481			if err != nil {
482				t.Fatalf("create %q: %v", ti, err)
483			}
484			ids[ti] = a.ID
485			time.Sleep(15 * time.Millisecond)
486		}
487		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, ids["second"], mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
488			t.Fatalf("pin: %v", err)
489		}
490	
491		got, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
492		if err != nil {
493			t.Fatalf("ListByBoard: %v", err)
494		}
495		if len(got) != 4 {
496			t.Fatalf("got %d articles, want 4", len(got))
497		}
498		// Expected order: pinned first ("second"), then unpinned by created_at DESC.
499		want := []string{"second", "fourth", "third", "first"}
500		for i, w := range want {
501			if got[i].Title != w {
502				t.Errorf("[%d] = %q, want %q (full order: %v)", i, got[i].Title, w, titlesOf(got))
503			}
504		}
505	
506		// Pin a second article — both should now be at the top, ordered by
507		// created_at DESC within the pinned group.
508		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, ids["fourth"], mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
509			t.Fatalf("pin fourth: %v", err)
510		}
511		got, _ = st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
512		wantMulti := []string{"fourth", "second", "third", "first"}
513		for i, w := range wantMulti {
514			if got[i].Title != w {
515				t.Errorf("multi-pin [%d] = %q, want %q (full order: %v)", i, got[i].Title, w, titlesOf(got))
516			}
517		}
518	}

520	func TestArticles_Update_PreservesPinnedAt(t *testing.T) {
521		ctx := context.Background()
522		st := storetest.New(t)
523		mod := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "mod", "")
524		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
525		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, mod.ID, mod.UserID, "rules v1", "body")
526		if err := st.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, mod.ID, store.RoleMod, true); err != nil {
527			t.Fatalf("pin: %v", err)
528		}
529		pinnedBefore, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
530		if !pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Valid {
531			t.Fatalf("pin precondition failed")
532		}
533		if err := st.Articles().Update(ctx, a.ID, mod.ID, store.RoleMod, "rules v2", "amended body"); err != nil {
534			t.Fatalf("Update: %v", err)
535		}
536		got, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, a.ID)
537		if !got.PinnedAt.Valid {
538			t.Errorf("PinnedAt cleared by Update — must be preserved")
539		}
540		if !got.PinnedAt.Time.Equal(pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Time) {
541			t.Errorf("PinnedAt mutated by Update: before=%v after=%v", pinnedBefore.PinnedAt.Time, got.PinnedAt.Time)
```

```text
34		case tea.KeyMsg:
35			switch msg.String() {

38			case "Q":

40			case "up", "k", "[":
41				if m.cursor > 0 {
42					m.cursor--
43				}
44			case "down", "j", "]":
45				if m.cursor < len(m.boards)-1 {
46					m.cursor++
47				}
48			case "home", "g":
49				m.cursor = 0
50			case "end", "G":
51				if len(m.boards) > 0 {
52					m.cursor = len(m.boards) - 1
53				}
54			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
55				if len(m.boards) == 0 {
56					return m, nil
57				}
58				b := m.boards[m.cursor]
59				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: b.ID} }
60			}
61		}
62		return m, nil
63	}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 09:13:37Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

type boardListModel struct {
	deps         Deps
	boards       []*store.Board // canonical full list from the store
	filtered     []*store.Board // boards visible after applying m.filter
	cursor       int            // index into filtered
	width        int
	height       int
	loadErr      error
	search       textinput.Model // input for the active search term
	searchActive bool            // true while focused & accepting keys
	filter       string          // confirmed (non-empty) substring; "" = no filter
}

func newBoardListModel(deps Deps) boardListModel {
	boards, err := deps.Store.Boards().List(context.Background())
	ti := textinput.New()
	ti.Placeholder = "搜尋看板 / search boards"
	ti.CharLimit = 32
	ti.Width = 40
	m := boardListModel{deps: deps, boards: boards, loadErr: err, search: ti}
	m.recomputeFilter()
	return m
}

func (m boardListModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }

// recomputeFilter rebuilds m.filtered from m.boards using the current
// m.filter (case-insensitive substring against name/title/description) and
// clamps the cursor so it never points past the end of the new slice.
// strings.ToLower is a no-op for CJK runes, which still match by byte
// substring — fine for the boards we ship today.
func (m *boardListModel) recomputeFilter() {
	if m.filter == "" {
		m.filtered = m.boards
	} else {
		lq := strings.ToLower(m.filter)
		out := make([]*store.Board, 0, len(m.boards))
		for _, b := range m.boards {
			if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Name), lq) ||
				strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Title), lq) ||
				strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(b.Description), lq) {
				out = append(out, b)
			}
		}
		m.filtered = out
	}
	if m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
		m.cursor = max(0, len(m.filtered)-1)
	}
}

func (m boardListModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
		m.search.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
		return m, nil
	case tea.KeyMsg:
		// While the search input is focused every printable key types into
		// it; only enter (confirm), esc (cancel), and ctrl+c (handled at
		// Root) escape that. This mirrors the form-screen rule from
		// CLAUDE.md so vim navigation keys (h/j/k/l) and action keys (Q)
		// remain available as text characters.
		if m.searchActive {
			switch msg.String() {
			case "enter":
				m.filter = strings.TrimSpace(m.search.Value())
				m.searchActive = false
				m.search.Blur()
				m.cursor = 0
				m.recomputeFilter()
				return m, nil
			case "esc":
				// Drop the in-flight value AND any prior confirmed filter.
				m.search.SetValue("")
				m.filter = ""
				m.searchActive = false
				m.search.Blur()
				m.cursor = 0
				m.recomputeFilter()
				return m, nil
			}
			var cmd tea.Cmd
			m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
			return m, cmd
		}

		switch msg.String() {
		case "/":
			m.searchActive = true
			m.search.SetValue(m.filter)
			m.search.CursorEnd()
			m.search.Focus()
			return m, textinput.Blink
		case "esc":
			// While idle, esc clears an active filter first; otherwise it
			// returns to the main menu (matches existing back-navigation).
			if m.filter != "" {
				m.filter = ""
				m.search.SetValue("")
				m.cursor = 0
				m.recomputeFilter()
				return m, nil
			}
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "backspace", "left", "h":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "Q":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "up", "k", "[":
			if m.cursor > 0 {
				m.cursor--
			}
		case "down", "j", "]":
			if m.cursor < len(m.filtered)-1 {
				m.cursor++
			}
		case "home", "g":
			m.cursor = 0
		case "end", "G":
			if len(m.filtered) > 0 {
				m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
			}
		case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
			if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
				return m, nil
			}
			b := m.filtered[m.cursor]
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: b.ID} }
		}
	}
	return m, nil
}

func (m boardListModel) View() string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString("\n")
	b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板列表 Boards "))
	b.WriteString("\n\n")

	if m.loadErr != nil {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ load failed: "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
		return b.String()
	}

	// Search row: live input above the list while focused; passive
	// "[搜尋: foo · N 筆 …]" indicator when a confirmed filter is active.
	switch {
	case m.searchActive:
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("搜尋 / : ") + m.search.View() + "\n")
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("(%d 筆符合 · Enter 套用 · Esc 取消)", len(m.filtered))) + "\n\n")
	case m.filter != "":
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[搜尋: %s · %d 筆 · / 修改 · Esc 清除]", m.filter, len(m.filtered))) + "\n\n")
	}

	if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
		if m.filter != "" {
			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(沒有符合的看板)") + "\n")
		} else {
			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no boards yet)") + "\n")
		}
		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(boardListFooter()) + "\n")
		return b.String()
	}

	const (
		nameW  = 14
		titleW = 32
	)
	header := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s  %s",
		PadRight("Name", nameW),
		PadRight("Title", titleW),
		"Description",
	)
	b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")

	for i, br := range m.filtered {
		row := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s  %s",
			PadRight(br.Name, nameW),
			PadRight(br.Title, titleW),
			Truncate(br.Description, max(20, m.width-nameW-titleW-8)),
		)
		if i == m.cursor {
			b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
		} else {
			b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
		}
	}

	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(boardListFooter()))
	b.WriteString("\n")
	return b.String()
}

func boardListFooter() string {
	return "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · / search · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
}

```

```text
126	func contains(s, sub string) bool {
127		return len(s) >= len(sub) && (s == sub || indexOf(s, sub) >= 0)
128	}

132			if s[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {

134			}
```

```text
1	package tui

11	// keyOf builds a tea.KeyMsg whose .String() matches the wanted form.
12	// bubbletea exposes typed key constants; we use Runes for ASCII letters
13	// and named Type values for special keys.
14	func keyOf(s string) tea.KeyMsg {
15		switch s {
16		case "enter":
17			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEnter}
18		case "esc":
19			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEsc}
20		case "left":
21			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyLeft}
22		case "right":
23			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRight}
24		case "up":
25			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyUp}
26		case "down":
27			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}
28		case "backspace":
29			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyBackspace}
30		case "ctrl+u":
31			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlU}
32		case "ctrl+c":
33			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlC}
34		case " ":
35			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeySpace}
36		}
37		return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune(s)}
38	}
39	
40	// runCmd invokes the tea.Cmd returned by Update and returns the resulting
41	// tea.Msg (or nil if cmd is nil).
42	func runCmd(cmd tea.Cmd) tea.Msg {
43		if cmd == nil {
44			return nil
45		}
46		return cmd()
47	}
48	
49	func newMainMenuFixture() (mainMenuModel, Deps) {
50		deps := Deps{
51			User: &store.User{ID: 1, UserID: "alice", Nickname: "Alice"},
52		}
53		return newMainMenuModel(deps), deps
54	}
55
```

```text
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	type boardViewModel struct {
14		deps     Deps
15		board    *store.Board
16		articles []*store.Article
17		cursor   int
18		width    int
19		height   int
20		loadErr  error
21	}
22	
23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case ArticlePinChangedMsg:
49			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
50				// Pin re-orders the list. Re-anchor the cursor by article ID so
51				// the same article stays highlighted across the reflow.
52				anchorID := int64(0)
53				if m.cursor < len(m.articles) {
54					anchorID = m.articles[m.cursor].ID
55				}
56				ctx := context.Background()
57				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
58					m.articles = arts
59					m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID)
60				}
61			}
62			return m, nil
63		case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
64			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
65				// Mode flip doesn't reorder, but we still re-anchor the cursor
66				// for parity with the pin handler — if a future change adds
67				// list ordering by comments_mode, we won't lose the highlight.
68				anchorID := int64(0)
69				if m.cursor < len(m.articles) {
70					anchorID = m.articles[m.cursor].ID
71				}
72				ctx := context.Background()
73				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
74					m.articles = arts
75					m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID)
76				}
77			}
78			return m, nil
79		case tea.KeyMsg:
80			switch msg.String() {
81			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
82				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
83			case "Q":
84				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
85			case "up", "k", "[":
86				if m.cursor > 0 {
87					m.cursor--
88				}
89			case "down", "j", "]":
90				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
91					m.cursor++
92				}
93			case "home", "g":
94				m.cursor = 0
95			case "end", "G":
96				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
97					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
98				}
99			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
100				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
101					return m, nil
102				}
103				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
104				return m, func() tea.Msg {
105					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
106				}
107			case "p":
108				if m.board == nil {
109					return m, nil
110				}
111				return m, func() tea.Msg {
112					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
113				}
114			case "b":
115				if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
116					return m, nil
117				}
118				return m, func() tea.Msg {
119					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardSplash, BoardID: m.board.ID}
120				}
121			case "B":
122				if m.board == nil || !m.canEditBanner() {
123					return m, nil
124				}
125				return m, func() tea.Msg {
126					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardBannerEdit, BoardID: m.board.ID}
127				}
128			case "M":
129				// Toggle pin on the cursor article. Mod+ only — silent no-op
130				// otherwise so non-mods don't get a confusing error toast for
131				// trying a key the help line doesn't even mention to them.
132				if m.board == nil || !m.canPin() || len(m.articles) == 0 {
133					return m, nil
134				}
135				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
136				pinned := !a.PinnedAt.Valid // toggle
137				ctx := context.Background()
138				u := m.deps.User
139				if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, u.ID, u.Role, pinned); err != nil {
140					return m, nil
141				}
142				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
143					m.articles = arts
144					m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, a.ID)
145				}
146				if m.deps.Broker != nil {
147					m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticlePinChangedMsg{
148						BoardID: m.board.ID, ArticleID: a.ID, Pinned: pinned,
149					})
150				}
151				return m, nil
152			}
153		}
154		return m, nil
155	}
156	
157	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
158		var b strings.Builder
159		b.WriteString("\n")
160		if m.board != nil {
161			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
162		} else {
163			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
164		}
165		b.WriteString("\n\n")
166		b.WriteString(m.renderBanner())
167	
168		if m.loadErr != nil {
169			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
170			return b.String()
171		}
172		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
173			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
174			help := "p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
175			if m.isGuest() {
176				hint = "(no articles yet)"
177				help = "Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
178			}
179			help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
180			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
181			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
182			return b.String()
183		}
184	
185		const (
186			idxW    = 4
187			dateW   = 11
188			scoreW  = 4
189			authorW = 14
190		)
191		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
192		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
193			PadRight("#", idxW),
194			PadRight("Date", dateW),
195			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
196			PadRight("Author", authorW),
197			PadRight("Title", titleW),
198		)
199		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
200	
201		for i, a := range m.articles {
202			score := ""
203			switch {
204			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
205				score = "爆"
206			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
207				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
208			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
209				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
210			}
211			// Pinned (板規 / 置頂) articles surface a leading "[M] " marker —
212			// the same glyph PTT uses for M-marked posts. Locked / arrows-only
213			// articles stack additional [鎖] / [箭] badges so users see the
214			// constraint before opening the article. Long titles still get
215			// truncated by Truncate.
216			title := a.Title
217			var prefix string
218			if a.PinnedAt.Valid {
219				prefix += "[M]"
220			}
221			switch a.CommentsMode {
222			case store.CommentsModeLocked:
223				prefix += "[鎖]"
224			case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
225				prefix += "[箭]"
226			}
227			if prefix != "" {
228				title = prefix + " " + a.Title
229			}
230			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
231				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
232				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
233				PadRight(score, scoreW),
234				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
235				Truncate(title, titleW),
236			)
237			if i == m.cursor {
238				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
239			} else {
240				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
241			}
242		}
243	
244		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
245		if m.isGuest() {
246			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
247		}
248		help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
249		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
250		b.WriteString("\n")
251		return b.String()
252	}
253	
254	func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
255		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
256	}
257	
258	func (m boardViewModel) canEditBanner() bool {
259		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
260	}
261	
262	// canPin gates the M shortcut. Mirrors canEditBanner — pinning is a
263	// moderation action and intentionally does NOT admit the article author
264	// (an author shouldn't bubble their own post to the top).
265	func (m boardViewModel) canPin() bool {
266		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
267	}
268	
269	// findCursorByID returns the index of the article with the given ID in
270	// the slice, or 0 if not found. Used to re-anchor the cursor after a
271	// pin/unpin reorders the list.
272	func findCursorByID(arts []*store.Article, id int64) int {
273		for i, a := range arts {
274			if a.ID == id {
275				return i
276			}
277		}
278		return 0
279	}
280	
281	// appendBannerHelp tacks "b banner" / "B edit" / "M pin" hints onto a
282	// help-line only when relevant: `b` requires a non-empty banner; `B` and
283	// `M` require mod+. Order matches the existing pattern (action keys
284	// before navigation). Name kept for git-blame stability — it now covers
285	// all mod-affordances on the board view, not just banner ones.
286	func (m boardViewModel) appendBannerHelp(help string) string {
287		if m.board != nil && m.board.Banner != "" {
288			help += " · b banner"
289		}
290		if m.canEditBanner() {
291			help += " · B edit"
292		}
293		if m.canPin() {
294			help += " · M pin/unpin"
295		}
296		return help
297	}
298	
299	const maxInlineBannerLines = 8
300	
301	// renderBanner produces the inline banner block shown above the article
302	// list. Empty string when the board has no banner. Each line is
303	// ANSI-aware-truncated to the viewport width with a 2-space left margin;
304	// the block is capped at maxInlineBannerLines with a "(press b)" hint
305	// when the source has more.
306	func (m boardViewModel) renderBanner() string {
307		if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
308			return ""
309		}
310		width := m.width - 2
311		if width < 20 {
312			// Before the first WindowSizeMsg lands m.width is 0; fall back to a
313			// generous default so the banner doesn't render as a single column.
314			width = 78
315		}
316		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimRight(m.board.Banner, "\n"), "\n")
317		limit := maxInlineBannerLines
318		truncated := false
319		if len(lines) > limit {
320			lines = lines[:limit]
321			truncated = true
322		}
323		var out strings.Builder
324		for _, line := range lines {
325			out.WriteString("  ")
326			out.WriteString(bannerTruncate(line, width))
327			out.WriteString("\n")
328		}
329		if truncated {
330			out.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("…(banner truncated — press b for full view)") + "\n")
331		}
332		out.WriteString("\n")
333		return out.String()
334	}
335
```

```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

type boardViewModel struct {
	deps         Deps
	board        *store.Board
	articles     []*store.Article
	cursor       int
	width        int
	height       int
	loadErr      error
	search       textinput.Model   // input for the active search term
	searchActive bool              // true while focused & accepting keys
	filter       string            // confirmed title substring; "" = no filter
	sort         store.ArticleSort // SortNewestFirst (default) or SortByScoreDesc
}

func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
	ctx := context.Background()
	board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
	if err != nil {
		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
	}
	ti := textinput.New()
	ti.Placeholder = "搜尋標題 / search title"
	ti.CharLimit = 64
	ti.Width = 50
	m := boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, search: ti}
	return m.reload(0)
}

func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }

// reload re-fetches articles using the current m.filter and m.sort and
// returns the updated model. anchorID > 0 keeps the cursor on the same
// article ID across the reflow; anchorID == 0 leaves the cursor at its
// numeric index (clamped to the new length). DB errors after the initial
// load are silent so a transient broadcast-driven refresh doesn't blank
// the screen — matches the pre-refactor behaviour of the inline reloads.
func (m boardViewModel) reload(anchorID int64) boardViewModel {
	if m.board == nil {
		return m
	}
	ctx := context.Background()
	arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoardOpts(ctx, m.board.ID,
		store.ListArticlesOpts{Limit: 100, TitleSearch: m.filter, Sort: m.sort})
	if err != nil {
		if m.loadErr == nil {
			m.loadErr = err
		}
		return m
	}
	m.articles = arts
	if anchorID > 0 {
		m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID)
	}
	if m.cursor >= len(arts) {
		m.cursor = max(0, len(arts)-1)
	}
	return m
}

// currentAnchorID returns the cursored article's ID, or 0 if the list is
// empty. Used to preserve the highlight across reloads triggered by sort
// toggles or pin/mode broadcasts.
func (m boardViewModel) currentAnchorID() int64 {
	if m.cursor < len(m.articles) {
		return m.articles[m.cursor].ID
	}
	return 0
}

func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
		m.search.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
		return m, nil
	case ArticleAddedMsg:
		if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
			// No anchor — preserve cursor index. Matches pre-refactor
			// behaviour: a freshly added article shifts the cursor to
			// what was previously one row below it (in newest-first sort).
			m = m.reload(0)
		}
		return m, nil
	case ArticlePinChangedMsg:
		if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
			// Pin re-orders the list — re-anchor the cursor by article ID
			// so the same article stays highlighted across the reflow.
			m = m.reload(m.currentAnchorID())
		}
		return m, nil
	case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
		if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
			m = m.reload(m.currentAnchorID())
		}
		return m, nil
	case tea.KeyMsg:
		// While the search input is focused every printable key types into
		// it; only enter (confirm), esc (cancel), and ctrl+c (handled at
		// Root) escape that. Action keys (s/M/p/b/B/Q) and vim navigation
		// (h/j/k/l/g/G/[/]) all become text characters during search.
		if m.searchActive {
			switch msg.String() {
			case "enter":
				m.filter = strings.TrimSpace(m.search.Value())
				m.searchActive = false
				m.search.Blur()
				m.cursor = 0
				m = m.reload(0)
				return m, nil
			case "esc":
				m.search.SetValue("")
				m.filter = ""
				m.searchActive = false
				m.search.Blur()
				m.cursor = 0
				m = m.reload(0)
				return m, nil
			}
			var cmd tea.Cmd
			m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
			return m, cmd
		}

		switch msg.String() {
		case "/":
			m.searchActive = true
			m.search.SetValue(m.filter)
			m.search.CursorEnd()
			m.search.Focus()
			return m, textinput.Blink
		case "esc":
			if m.filter != "" {
				m.filter = ""
				m.search.SetValue("")
				m.cursor = 0
				m = m.reload(0)
				return m, nil
			}
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
		case "backspace", "left", "h":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
		case "Q":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "s":
			// Cycle sort. Anchor on the cursored article so the highlight
			// follows the row across the reorder. NOTE(score-vs-pushcount):
			// the user-facing label says 推文量 (push amount); under the hood
			// we sort by recommend_score (signed +1/-1/0 sum). Acceptable
			// proxy for now; if the product distinction matters later, add
			// a maintained push_count column.
			m.sort = (m.sort + 1) % 2
			m = m.reload(m.currentAnchorID())
			return m, nil
		case "up", "k", "[":
			if m.cursor > 0 {
				m.cursor--
			}
		case "down", "j", "]":
			if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
				m.cursor++
			}
		case "home", "g":
			m.cursor = 0
		case "end", "G":
			if len(m.articles) > 0 {
				m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
			}
		case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
			if len(m.articles) == 0 {
				return m, nil
			}
			a := m.articles[m.cursor]
			return m, func() tea.Msg {
				return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
			}
		case "p":
			if m.board == nil {
				return m, nil
			}
			return m, func() tea.Msg {
				return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
			}
		case "b":
			if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
				return m, nil
			}
			return m, func() tea.Msg {
				return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardSplash, BoardID: m.board.ID}
			}
		case "B":
			if m.board == nil || !m.canEditBanner() {
				return m, nil
			}
			return m, func() tea.Msg {
				return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardBannerEdit, BoardID: m.board.ID}
			}
		case "M":
			// Toggle pin on the cursor article. Mod+ only — silent no-op
			// otherwise so non-mods don't get a confusing error toast for
			// trying a key the help line doesn't even mention to them.
			if m.board == nil || !m.canPin() || len(m.articles) == 0 {
				return m, nil
			}
			a := m.articles[m.cursor]
			pinned := !a.PinnedAt.Valid
			ctx := context.Background()
			u := m.deps.User
			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, u.ID, u.Role, pinned); err != nil {
				return m, nil
			}
			m = m.reload(a.ID)
			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticlePinChangedMsg{
					BoardID: m.board.ID, ArticleID: a.ID, Pinned: pinned,
				})
			}
			return m, nil
		}
	}
	return m, nil
}

func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString("\n")
	if m.board != nil {
		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
	} else {
		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
	}
	b.WriteString("\n\n")
	b.WriteString(m.renderBanner())

	if m.loadErr != nil {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
		return b.String()
	}

	// Search row + sort indicator. Search runs above the article table so the
	// table's row layout stays predictable across show/hide.
	switch {
	case m.searchActive:
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("搜尋 / 標題: ") + m.search.View() + "\n")
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(Enter 套用 · Esc 取消)") + "\n\n")
	case m.filter != "":
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[搜尋: %s · %d 筆 · / 修改 · Esc 清除]", m.filter, len(m.articles))) + "\n\n")
	}

	if len(m.articles) == 0 {
		hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
		help := "p post · / search · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
		if m.filter != "" {
			hint = "(沒有符合的文章)"
		}
		if m.isGuest() {
			hint = "(no articles yet)"
			help = "/ search · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
		}
		help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
		return b.String()
	}

	const (
		idxW    = 4
		dateW   = 11
		scoreW  = 4
		authorW = 14
	)
	titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
	header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
		PadRight("#", idxW),
		PadRight("Date", dateW),
		PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
		PadRight("Author", authorW),
		PadRight("Title", titleW),
	)
	b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header))
	if m.sort == store.SortByScoreDesc {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("[排序: 推文量↓]"))
	}
	b.WriteString("\n")

	for i, a := range m.articles {
		score := ""
		switch {
		case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
			score = "爆"
		case a.RecommendScore > 0:
			score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
		case a.RecommendScore < 0:
			score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
		}
		// Pinned (板規 / 置頂) articles surface a leading "[M] " marker —
		// the same glyph PTT uses for M-marked posts. Locked / arrows-only
		// articles stack additional [鎖] / [箭] badges so users see the
		// constraint before opening the article. Long titles still get
		// truncated by Truncate.
		title := a.Title
		var prefix string
		if a.PinnedAt.Valid {
			prefix += "[M]"
		}
		switch a.CommentsMode {
		case store.CommentsModeLocked:
			prefix += "[鎖]"
		case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
			prefix += "[箭]"
		}
		if prefix != "" {
			title = prefix + " " + a.Title
		}
		row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
			PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
			PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
			PadRight(score, scoreW),
			PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
			Truncate(title, titleW),
		)
		if i == m.cursor {
			b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
		} else {
			b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
		}
	}

	help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · / search · s sort · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
	if m.isGuest() {
		help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · / search · s sort · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
	}
	help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
	b.WriteString("\n")
	return b.String()
}

func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
	return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
}

func (m boardViewModel) canEditBanner() bool {
	return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
}

// canPin gates the M shortcut. Mirrors canEditBanner — pinning is a
// moderation action and intentionally does NOT admit the article author
// (an author shouldn't bubble their own post to the top).
func (m boardViewModel) canPin() bool {
	return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
}

// findCursorByID returns the index of the article with the given ID in
// the slice, or 0 if not found. Used to re-anchor the cursor after a
// pin/unpin reorders the list.
func findCursorByID(arts []*store.Article, id int64) int {
	for i, a := range arts {
		if a.ID == id {
			return i
		}
	}
	return 0
}

// appendBannerHelp tacks "b banner" / "B edit" / "M pin" hints onto a
// help-line only when relevant: `b` requires a non-empty banner; `B` and
// `M` require mod+. Order matches the existing pattern (action keys
// before navigation). Name kept for git-blame stability — it now covers
// all mod-affordances on the board view, not just banner ones.
func (m boardViewModel) appendBannerHelp(help string) string {
	if m.board != nil && m.board.Banner != "" {
		help += " · b banner"
	}
	if m.canEditBanner() {
		help += " · B edit"
	}
	if m.canPin() {
		help += " · M pin/unpin"
	}
	return help
}

const maxInlineBannerLines = 8

// renderBanner produces the inline banner block shown above the article
// list. Empty string when the board has no banner. Each line is
// ANSI-aware-truncated to the viewport width with a 2-space left margin;
// the block is capped at maxInlineBannerLines with a "(press b)" hint
// when the source has more.
func (m boardViewModel) renderBanner() string {
	if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
		return ""
	}
	width := m.width - 2
	if width < 20 {
		// Before the first WindowSizeMsg lands m.width is 0; fall back to a
		// generous default so the banner doesn't render as a single column.
		width = 78
	}
	lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimRight(m.board.Banner, "\n"), "\n")
	limit := maxInlineBannerLines
	truncated := false
	if len(lines) > limit {
		lines = lines[:limit]
		truncated = true
	}
	var out strings.Builder
	for _, line := range lines {
		out.WriteString("  ")
		out.WriteString(bannerTruncate(line, width))
		out.WriteString("\n")
	}
	if truncated {
		out.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("…(banner truncated — press b for full view)") + "\n")
	}
	out.WriteString("\n")
	return out.String()
}

```

```text
1	package tui
```

```text
10	// HelpEntry is one row in a screen's keymap. Keys is the displayed shortcut
11	// (e.g. "Enter/→/l"), Desc the human-readable action.
12	type HelpEntry struct {
13		Keys string
14		Desc string
15	}
16	
17	// helpHeader is the optional title row shown above the keymap. Empty for
18	// most screens; some screens use it to clarify mode-specific keys.
19	type helpSection struct {
20		Heading string
21		Entries []HelpEntry
22	}
23	
24	// screenHelp maps a Screen constant to the keys we want the help overlay
25	// to surface. Mirrors the inline help-line strings rendered at the bottom
26	// of each screen, with extra notes on globals (Ctrl+U, Ctrl+C, ?) so the
27	// overlay is self-contained.
28	//
29	// Form screens (compose, edit, password change, board-banner edit) are
30	// intentionally absent: they need to pass `?` through to textinput so the
31	// user can type it as a literal character. Their bottom help-line already
32	// surfaces every key they bind.
33	var screenHelp = map[Screen][]helpSection{
34		ScreenMainMenu: {{
35			Heading: "Main menu",
36			Entries: []HelpEntry{

62			},
63		}},

65			Heading: "Article view",
66			Entries: []HelpEntry{
67				{"j/k", "scroll"},
68				{"g/G", "top / bottom"},

70				{"+ / - / =", "推 / 噓 / → (non-guest)"},
71				{"p / P", "select push for delete"},

145			Entries: []HelpEntry{

147				{"[ / ]", "prev / next page"},
148				{"a / m / u / g", "promote to admin / mod / user / guest"},
149				{"R", "reset password (forces change on next login)"},
150				{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
151			},
152		}},

154			Heading: "Export article",
155			Entries: []HelpEntry{
156				{"1", "plain body"},
157				{"2", "body + pushes"},
158				{"3", "save to data/exports/<userid>/"},
159				{"c", "OSC52 copy to clipboard"},
160				{"Esc/←/h/q", "back"},
161			},
162		}},
163		ScreenBoardSplash: {{
164			Heading: "Board banner splash",
165			Entries: []HelpEntry{
166				{"any key", "return to board view"},
167			},
168		}},
169	}
170	
171	// helpGlobals are the cross-screen keys shown on every help page so users
172	// don't need to discover them per screen.
173	var helpGlobals = []HelpEntry{
174		{"?", "show this help (any key dismisses)"},
175		{"Ctrl+U", "open 水球 inbox (logged-in only)"},
176		{"Ctrl+C", "disconnect"},
177	}
178	
179	// isFormScreen reports whether a screen is a text-entry form whose
180	// textinput / textarea must receive '?' as a literal character. The help
181	// overlay is suppressed on these screens; the bottom help-line each form
182	// already renders is the source of truth there.
183	func isFormScreen(s Screen) bool {
184		switch s {
185		case ScreenPostCompose,
186			ScreenWBCompose,
187			ScreenMailCompose,
188			ScreenArticleEdit,
189			ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
190			ScreenPasswordChange:
191			return true
192		}
193		return false
194	}
195	
196	// helpAvailable reports whether the help overlay should respond to '?' on
197	// this screen. False for form screens and during register/MustChangePassword
198	// (those flows start before any Screen const is meaningful).
199	func helpAvailable(deps Deps, s Screen) bool {
200		if deps.IsRegister || deps.MustChangePassword {
201			return false
202		}
203		return !isFormScreen(s)
204	}
205	
206	// helpBox is the lipgloss frame around the help body. Width is set per-call
207	// so the box hugs the rendered content rather than spanning the terminal.
208	var helpBox = lipgloss.NewStyle().
209		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
210		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("63")).
211		Padding(1, 2)
212	
213	// renderHelp builds the full-screen help overlay for the given screen.
214	// Includes per-screen entries (or a fallback message) plus the globals.
215	func renderHelp(s Screen) string {
216		var b strings.Builder
217		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 鍵盤捷徑說明 Help ") + "\n\n")
218	
219		sections, ok := screenHelp[s]
220		if !ok {
221			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no per-screen keymap registered for this screen)") + "\n\n")
222		} else {
223			for i, sec := range sections {
224				if i > 0 {
225					b.WriteString("\n")
226				}
227				if sec.Heading != "" {
228					b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  "+sec.Heading) + "\n")
229				}
230				for _, e := range sec.Entries {
231					b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
232				}
233			}
234			b.WriteString("\n")
235		}
236	
237		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  Global") + "\n")
238		for _, e := range helpGlobals {
239			b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
240		}
241	
242		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to dismiss"))
243	
244		return helpBox.Render(b.String())
245	}
246	
247	// formatHelpRow pads keys to a stable column width so the descriptions
248	// align — uses the runewidth-aware PadRight because keys often contain
249	// arrows / CJK glyphs.
250	func formatHelpRow(e HelpEntry) string {
251		const keysW = 22
252		return fmt.Sprintf("    %s  %s", PadRight(e.Keys, keysW), e.Desc)
253	}
254
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"testing"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
12	)
13	
14	// rootForHelp builds a minimal Root logged in as alice on the main menu.
15	// Used by every help-overlay test below.
16	func rootForHelp(t *testing.T) Root {
17		t.Helper()
18		st := storetest.New(t)
19		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
20		deps := Deps{Store: st, User: alice, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
21		return NewRoot(deps)
22	}
23	
24	// TestHelp_QuestionMarkOpensOverlay asserts '?' on the main menu flips
25	// helpVisible and that View() returns the help block instead of the menu.
26	func TestHelp_QuestionMarkOpensOverlay(t *testing.T) {
27		r := rootForHelp(t)
28		if r.helpVisible {
29			t.Fatal("helpVisible should start false")
30		}
31		updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
32		r = updated.(Root)
33		if !r.helpVisible {
34			t.Errorf("? did not flip helpVisible")
35		}
36		v := r.View()
37		if !strings.Contains(v, "Help") {
38			t.Errorf("View missing help title; got: %q", v)
39		}
40		if !strings.Contains(v, "Main menu") {
41			t.Errorf("View missing main-menu section heading; got: %q", v)
42		}
43		if !strings.Contains(v, "? ") || !strings.Contains(v, "Global") {
44			t.Errorf("View missing global section / '?' entry; got: %q", v)
45		}
46	}
47	
48	// TestHelp_AnyKeyDismisses fires a non-'?' key while help is open and
49	// expects helpVisible to flip back off without triggering navigation.
50	func TestHelp_AnyKeyDismisses(t *testing.T) {
51		r := rootForHelp(t)
52		updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
53		r = updated.(Root)
54		if !r.helpVisible {
55			t.Fatal("setup: ? should open help")
56		}
57	
58		for _, key := range []string{"j", "esc", "enter", " ", "x"} {
59			t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
60				r2 := r // copy
61				r2.helpVisible = true
62				updated, cmd := r2.Update(keyOf(key))
63				r2 = updated.(Root)
64				if r2.helpVisible {
65					t.Errorf("key %q did not dismiss help overlay", key)
66				}
67				if cmd != nil {
68					if msg := runCmd(cmd); msg != nil {
69						t.Errorf("dismissing key %q produced %T %+v, want nil", key, msg, msg)
70					}
71				}
72			})
73		}
74	}
75	
76	// TestHelp_CtrlCStillQuitsWhileOpen guards against a regression where the
77	// help overlay swallows Ctrl+C. Ctrl+C must always quit.
78	func TestHelp_CtrlCStillQuitsWhileOpen(t *testing.T) {
79		r := rootForHelp(t)
80		updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
81		r = updated.(Root)
82		if !r.helpVisible {
83			t.Fatal("setup")
84		}
85		_, cmd := r.Update(keyOf("ctrl+c"))
86		if cmd == nil {
87			t.Fatal("ctrl+c returned no cmd")
88		}
89		got := runCmd(cmd)
90		if _, ok := got.(tea.QuitMsg); !ok {
91			t.Errorf("ctrl+c → %T %+v, want tea.QuitMsg", got, got)
92		}
93	}
94	
95	// TestHelp_ActiveScreenTrackedAcrossNavigate asserts navigate updates the
96	// activeScreen field so subsequent '?' shows the correct page.
97	func TestHelp_ActiveScreenTrackedAcrossNavigate(t *testing.T) {
98		r := rootForHelp(t)
99		if r.activeScreen != ScreenMainMenu {
100			t.Errorf("initial activeScreen = %v, want ScreenMainMenu", r.activeScreen)
101		}
102		updated, _ := r.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
103		r = updated.(Root)
104		if r.activeScreen != ScreenWBInbox {
105			t.Errorf("after navigate to ScreenWBInbox: activeScreen = %v", r.activeScreen)
106		}
107	
108		// '?' on the WB inbox should mention water balloons.
109		updated, _ = r.Update(keyOf("?"))
110		r = updated.(Root)
111		if !strings.Contains(r.View(), "水球") {
112			t.Errorf("WB inbox help missing 水球 heading; got: %q", r.View())
113		}
114	}
115	
116	// TestHelp_FormScreensSuppressOverlay verifies '?' is forwarded to the
117	// sub-screen (not intercepted) when a form screen is active. We assert
118	// helpVisible stays false; the textinput within the sub will receive the
119	// keypress and treat it as input.
120	func TestHelp_FormScreensSuppressOverlay(t *testing.T) {
121		formScreens := []Screen{
122			ScreenPostCompose,
123			ScreenWBCompose,
124			ScreenMailCompose,
125			ScreenArticleEdit,
126			ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
```

Base directory for this skill: /Users/daviddwlee84/.claude/skills/agent-history-hygiene

# agent-history-hygiene

Keep agent chat transcripts and plan files committed together with the
code they produced, without leaking secrets. Pairs with the
`redact-agent-secrets` + `gitleaks` pre-commit hooks the skill installs.

Three surfaces, separated by purpose:

| Surface                          | Question it answers                                          |
|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `find-session.sh`                | "Which transcript / plan file is *my* current session?"      |
| `stage-agent-artifacts.sh`       | "Which agent files belong in the next commit?"               |
| `bootstrap-project.sh`           | "How do I get pre-commit + gitleaks + redactor into a repo?" |
| `scan-staged.sh`                 | "Is there a leaked secret in what I'm about to commit?"      |
| `references/remediation.md`      | "I already pushed a secret — now what?"                      |

## Core invariants

1. **Agent transcripts and plan files are committed alongside the diff
   that produced them.** Never add them to `.gitignore`. An agent that
   drops these from a commit has broken the user's review trail.
2. **Rotate at the provider before any git rewrite.** The only act
   that revokes a leaked credential is rotation. History rewriting
   scrubs bytes on one clone and leaves them on every other.
3. **`git push --force` against a shared branch is never the fix for a
   leak.** At best it's useless; at worst it destroys teammate work and
   silently re-introduces the secret when someone merges their old
   history back.

## When to use this skill

Use it when the user (or you) surface any of:

- "Commit my chat" / "save the specstory session" / "include the plan
  file in this commit" / "把 plan 跟 specstory 一起 commit 進去".
- You see dirty `.specstory/history/*.md`, `.claude/plans/*.md`,
  `.cursor/plans/*.md`, or any other configured agent artifact during
  `git status` and you're about to commit a feature.
- "Scrub this transcript" / "redact my key" / "gitleaks flagged my
  chat history".
- "Set up pre-commit for this repo" / "I'm starting a new project — how
  do I get the hook stack?" / "bootstrap secret scanning here".
- "I pushed a `.env`" / "a secret went to main" / "do I need to force
  push?" — the agent must steer to `references/remediation.md` and
  stop the user from force-pushing reflexively.

## When NOT to use

- The user explicitly wants agent transcripts **excluded** from the
  repo. Respect that; suggest a one-liner `.gitignore` addition and
  skip this skill entirely.
- The leak is already on a shared `main`/release branch. Do **not**
  offer to rewrite history — jump to `references/remediation.md` §5.
- The project genuinely has no agent session (no `.specstory/`, no
  `.claude/plans/`, etc.). Nothing to stage.
- Single-file, single-commit hygiene that the agent handles without any
  script (e.g., adding a missing trailing newline).

## Integration with existing infrastructure

This skill sits **on top of** any chezmoi-managed stack the user
already has. It does not duplicate:

- **chezmoi's global `core.hooksPath`** (`~/.config/git/hooks/pre-commit`)
  — that wrapper runs the repo's `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and then
  optionally `gitleaks git --staged`. The skill bootstraps the repo-level
  config the wrapper expects to find.
- **chezmoi's `.gitleaks.toml`** — the user's config already carries
  curated rules for common API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Supabase,
  Linear, WakaTime, Cursor, HuggingFace, Notion, Tailscale, Clash /
  V2Ray tokens). The skill's `assets/gitleaks.toml.template` ships the
  same rule IDs so `.gitleaksignore` / allowlist tweaks stay portable.
- **chezmoi's `scripts/redact_secrets.py`** — remains the upstream
  source of truth. The skill bundles a copy so non-chezmoi users get
  protection; sync procedure in
  [`references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md`](references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md).

What this skill **adds**:

- Agent-facing discipline (this `SKILL.md` + `references/remediation.md`).
- A single-command project bootstrap (`bootstrap-project.sh`) for repos
  without chezmoi or where the user wants the stack in one go.
- Session-discovery heuristics (`find-session.sh`) for the "find my
  current transcript among many" problem.
- An exit-code wrapper (`scan-staged.sh`) agents can branch on before
  committing.

## Workflow A: commit-time hygiene

Default flow when the agent is about to commit feature changes plus
chat/plan artifacts.

```bash
# 1. Make sure the agent knows which session is "ours" — mostly
#    relevant when multiple Claude/SpecStory sessions run in the repo.
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/find-session.sh

# 2. Stage code the usual way, then auto-add agent artifacts.
git add path/to/feature/file.ts
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/stage-agent-artifacts.sh
# Use --session-only if you want ONLY the current SpecStory + newest plan;
# default stages every dirty *.md in every configured agent dir.

# 3. Belt-and-suspenders secret scan before commit. Exit 0 = clean.
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/scan-staged.sh || {
  # Exit 10/20: leaks found. Jump to references/remediation.md.
  echo "Leaks detected — see references/remediation.md before committing." >&2
  exit 1
}

# 4. Commit. pre-commit (installed by bootstrap) runs redact-agent-secrets
#    then gitleaks again as a catch-all.
git commit -m "feat: ..."
```

## Workflow B: bootstrap a new project

For repos that don't yet have `.pre-commit-config.yaml` / `.gitleaks.toml`
installed. Runs once per repo.

```bash
cd /path/to/new/project
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/bootstrap-project.sh \
  --install-hook            # optional: auto-stage on every commit

# Verify: shake out any existing issues in the working tree.
pre-commit run --all-files
```

What `bootstrap-project.sh` does:

1. Drops `.pre-commit-config.yaml` + `.gitleaks.toml` into the repo
   (skips if already present unless `--force`).
2. Writes `scripts/redact_secrets.py` (bundled copy; use
   `--from-chezmoi` to symlink the chezmoi source so fixes propagate).
3. Runs `pre-commit install` (or `uvx pre-commit@4 install` if
   pre-commit isn't on `PATH`).
4. Audits `.gitignore` / `.git/info/exclude` for patterns that would
   silently hide an agent artifact dir — warns without editing.
5. Checks `~/.claude/settings.json` for `plansDirectory`; prints the
   one-line patch if missing.
6. With `--install-hook`: writes a `prepare-commit-msg` hook that calls
   `stage-agent-artifacts.sh --session-only --allow-empty` so every
   `git commit` auto-attaches the current session file.

## Workflow C: post-leak remediation

When `scan-staged.sh` reports exit `10`/`20`, or the user says "I
committed / pushed a secret":

1. **STOP** the user from running `git push --force` reflexively.
2. Read `references/remediation.md` end-to-end.
3. Walk the user through **step 1 (rotate)** regardless of blast
   radius. Only after rotation does the question of scrubbing history
   become worth discussing.
4. Use the decision tree in the runbook to pick the right git action.

## Gotchas

- **`plansDirectory` project-level sometimes ignored.** Claude Code
  issue [#19537](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/19537)
  reports project-level `plansDirectory` being ignored in some
  versions. After running a `/plan`, verify the file actually landed
  where you expected before relying on `stage-agent-artifacts.sh`
  picking it up. User-level config (`~/.claude/settings.json` with
  `"plansDirectory": "./.claude/plans"`) is the recommended default.
- **`gitleaks protect` is deprecated.** Since v8.19.0 use
  `gitleaks git --staged --redact` (pre-commit) and
  `gitleaks dir <path>` (working directory). The older commands still
  work but emit a deprecation notice. This skill uses the modern
  syntax everywhere.
- **`pre-commit install` is per-clone.** Each teammate must run
  `pre-commit install` in their own clone for hooks to fire. CI cannot
  be trusted as the single gate — it's second-chance, not last-chance.
- **Transcript files can be huge.** A long SpecStory session can exceed
  2 MB. The template bumps `check-added-large-files` to `--maxkb=2048`
  to avoid false positives, but a very long session can still overflow.
  If you hit the limit, rotate sessions (`specstory run claude`
  creates a fresh file) instead of raising the cap further.
- **Session-UUID divergence between SpecStory CLI and VS Code
  extension.** The extension autosaves into `.specstory/history/`
  continuously; the CLI (`specstory run claude`) creates one file per
  invocation. If both are active you can end up with two transcripts
  for what feels like "one session" — mtime-newest wins in
  `find-session.sh`.
- **Global `core.hooksPath` means bare repos aren't protected.** The
  chezmoi setup's global hook runs `.pre-commit-config.yaml` IF it
  exists — so a repo without `.pre-commit-config.yaml` has no
  protection. Run `bootstrap-project.sh` before the first commit with
  agent artifacts, not after.
- **Active SpecStory writer can defeat the redact loop.** The standard
  `git add → git commit → pre-commit auto-fixes → re-stage → re-commit`
  flow assumes the file is **quiescent** during the commit. SpecStory's
  `specstory_*_watch` daemon tails the agent transcript continuously,
  so if the chat captured `ps -axo args`-style output that contained an
  unrelated daemon's secret in argv (e.g. SpecStory's own
  `--cloud-token …` flag), every diagnostic command (`grep`, `sed -n
  '<line>p'`, `cat | head | tail`) prints the secret again, SpecStory
  appends it to the transcript, and the redact-then-restage cycle
  never converges. Symptom: pre-commit says "Successfully redacted N
  file(s)" but `gitleaks-system` immediately fails on the same line,
  re-running `git add && git commit` doesn't help, and `grep -c
  '<secret-prefix>' file` shows the count *increasing* over commit
  attempts. **Workaround**: a single atomic
  `python3 -c "<in-place re.sub>" && git add <file> && git commit -m
  "..."` pipeline so the index is frozen before any new specstory write
  lands. **Don't** print, grep, or diff the secret line during the
  recovery — every print echoes back into the transcript. Diagnose with
  `lsof <file>` (looking for `specstory_*` writers) instead. See
  `pitfalls/redact-secrets-loop-with-active-specstory-writer.md` in
  upstream chezmoi for the full debugging trail.

## Available scripts

- **`scripts/find-session.sh [--format=specstory|claude|both] [--json]`**
  Discover the current agent session files for `$PWD`. TSV default,
  `--json` for structured callers. Never exits non-zero (always 0,
  empty fields signal absence).

- **`scripts/stage-agent-artifacts.sh [--session-only] [--include-all-plans] [--dry-run] [--allow-empty]`**
  `git add` the right agent artifacts before the next commit.
  `--session-only` stages only the current SpecStory + newest plan;
  default stages every dirty `*.md` in every configured artifact dir.
  Refuses to run if there are no code changes (prevents "commit just
  transcript"); override with `--allow-empty`.

- **`scripts/scan-staged.sh [--redact] [--verbose]`**
  Run `gitleaks git --staged` with agent-friendly exit codes
  (0 clean / 10 redacted / 20 leaks / 30 gitleaks missing). JSON lines
  on stdout, prose diagnostics on stderr.

- **`scripts/bootstrap-project.sh [--from-chezmoi] [--install-hook] [--force] [--dry-run]`**
  Install `.pre-commit-config.yaml` + `.gitleaks.toml` + the redactor
  into the current repo, then run `pre-commit install`. Audits
  `.gitignore` and `~/.claude/settings.json` for misconfigurations
  (warns, never silently edits).

## Bundled assets

- `assets/artifact-dirs.txt` — the canonical list of agent artifact
  directories (SpecStory, Claude plans, Cursor plans + rules, OpenCode
  plans, Spec-kit, Codex). Consumed by `stage-agent-artifacts.sh` and
  by `bootstrap-project.sh` when rendering the pre-commit `files:`
  regex.
- `assets/pre-commit-config.yaml.template` — minimal
  `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with `redact-agent-secrets` + gitleaks +
  standard hygiene hooks.
- `assets/gitleaks.toml.template` — portable subset of the chezmoi
  `.gitleaks.toml` with custom rule IDs + a path-scoped allowlist for
  agent artifact dirs.
- `assets/redact_secrets.py` — bundled copy of chezmoi's
  `scripts/redact_secrets.py`. Chezmoi version is upstream; sync doc
  in `references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md`.

## Reference files

- [`references/transcript-session-discovery.md`](references/transcript-session-discovery.md)
  — SpecStory / Claude session layouts and the `$PWD → slug` algorithm.
  Read when `find-session.sh` returns empty or ambiguous results.
- [`references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md`](references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md)
  — three-layer defense (redact → gitleaks → `scan-staged.sh`),
  allowlist design, sync procedure for the bundled redactor. Read
  when tuning rules or debugging unexpected pre-commit failures.
- [`references/remediation.md`](references/remediation.md) —
  rotate-first runbook for "I committed / pushed a secret". Read
  **before** any `git filter-repo` / `git push --force` action.

## Tests

The skill ships with a three-level test suite under
[`tests/`](tests/README.md). Run from repo root:

```bash
make test-skill
```

- `test_redact_secrets.py` — pytest for pure redactor functions.
- `test_gitleaks_corpus.py` — golden-corpus fixtures staged in tmp git
  repos, asserting real-key shapes fire and example shapes are
  allowlisted only inside configured artifact dirs.
- `test_scan_staged.sh` — exit-code contract for
  `scripts/scan-staged.sh` (0 / 20 / 30 / 2).

The corpus + shell tests skip gracefully when `gitleaks` isn't on
`PATH`. See [`tests/README.md`](tests/README.md) for what each
regression the suite locks in.

## Related skills

- [`project-knowledge-harness`](../project-knowledge-harness/SKILL.md)
  — complementary memory harness (TODO.md + backlog/ + pitfalls/) that
  references `.claude/plans/` as "ephemeral agent scratchpads". This
  skill fills the gap: those scratchpads belong in git, not ignored.

```text
package store

	ID             int64
	BoardID        int64
	AuthorID       int64
	AuthorUserID   string
	Title          string
	Body           string
	RecommendScore int64
	Filemode       int64
	CreatedAt      time.Time
	UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
	PinnedAt       sql.NullTime
}

type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }

var (
	ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
	ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
)

const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
	recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at`

func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
	a := &Article{}
	err := row.Scan(
		&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
		&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
	)
	return a, err
}

func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
	if limit <= 0 {
		limit = 50
	}
	// Pinned articles surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1);
	// within each group, newest first by created_at then id (the latter
	// disambiguates rows that share a second-resolution timestamp).
	rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
```

```text
11	// CommentsMode controls who can leave 推/噓/→ pushes on an article. The
12	// values are persisted as TEXT in articles.comments_mode (see migration
13	// 0008). States are mutually exclusive — 'locked' is strictly stronger
14	// than 'arrows_only', so a single-column enum keeps the precedence rule
15	// unambiguous. Mod-only mutator: ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
16	type CommentsMode string
17	
18	const (
19		CommentsModeOpen       CommentsMode = "open"        // default — push, boo, arrow all allowed
20		CommentsModeArrowsOnly CommentsMode = "arrows_only" // only neutral arrow comments
21		CommentsModeLocked     CommentsMode = "locked"      // all pushes rejected
22	)
23	
24	func (m CommentsMode) Valid() bool {
25		switch m {
26		case CommentsModeOpen, CommentsModeArrowsOnly, CommentsModeLocked:
27			return true
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	type Article struct {
33		ID             int64
34		BoardID        int64
35		AuthorID       int64
36		AuthorUserID   string
37		Title          string
38		Body           string
39		RecommendScore int64

44		CommentsMode   CommentsMode
45	}
46	
47	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
48	
49	// ArticleSort selects the secondary ordering inside ListByBoardOpts. Pinned
50	// articles always float to the top regardless of sort — pin is an explicit
51	// moderation signal stronger than score or recency.
52	type ArticleSort int
53	
54	const (
55		SortNewestFirst ArticleSort = iota // default: created_at DESC, id DESC
56		SortByScoreDesc                    // recommend_score DESC, then created_at DESC, id DESC tiebreaker
57	)
58	
59	// ListArticlesOpts parameterises ListByBoardOpts. Zero value behaves
60	// identically to the legacy ListByBoard(boardID, 0): newest-first, no title
61	// filter, default 50-row limit.
62	type ListArticlesOpts struct {
63		Limit       int         // <=0 → 50
64		TitleSearch string      // empty → no filter; matched as LIKE '%q%' COLLATE NOCASE
65		Sort        ArticleSort
66	}
67	
68	// likeEscape protects user input from being interpreted as LIKE wildcards.
69	// Paired with `ESCAPE '\\'` in the SQL.
70	var likeEscape = strings.NewReplacer(`\`, `\\`, `%`, `\%`, `_`, `\_`)
71	
72	var (
73		ErrArticleNotFound     = errors.New("article not found")
74		ErrPermissionDenied    = errors.New("permission denied")
75		ErrInvalidCommentsMode = errors.New("invalid comments mode")
76	)
77	
78	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
79		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at, comments_mode`
80	
81	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
82		a := &Article{}
83		var commentsMode string
84		err := row.Scan(
85			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
86			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
87			&commentsMode,
88		)
89		a.CommentsMode = CommentsMode(commentsMode)
90		return a, err
91	}
92	
93	// ListByBoard returns the most-recent articles in a board, pinned first.
94	// Thin shim over ListByBoardOpts kept so existing call sites and tests
95	// (TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst, _Limit, _PinnedFirst) continue to
96	// exercise the legacy default behaviour as a regression guard.
97	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
98		return r.ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, ListArticlesOpts{Limit: limit})
99	}
100	
101	// ListByBoardOpts is the parameterised list endpoint that supports a
102	// case-insensitive title substring filter and an alternate score-DESC sort.
103	//
104	// Pinned articles always surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1).
105	// Within each pinned/non-pinned group the secondary ordering depends on
106	// opts.Sort:
107	//   - SortNewestFirst:  created_at DESC, id DESC
108	//   - SortByScoreDesc:  recommend_score DESC, then created_at DESC, id DESC
109	//
110	// The created_at/id tiebreaker is essential in score mode because many fresh
111	// posts share recommend_score=0 and need a deterministic secondary order.
112	//
113	// TODO(score-vs-pushcount): SortByScoreDesc orders by recommend_score (signed
114	// sum: +1 push, -1 boo, 0 arrow). A future "raw 推文量" sort would need a
115	// separate maintained push_count column.
116	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoardOpts(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, opts ListArticlesOpts) ([]*Article, error) {
117		limit := opts.Limit
118		if limit <= 0 {
119			limit = 50
120		}
121	
122		var (
123			where = "WHERE board_id = ?"
124			args  = []any{boardID}
125		)
126		if q := strings.TrimSpace(opts.TitleSearch); q != "" {
127			where += ` AND title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\' COLLATE NOCASE`
128			args = append(args, "%"+likeEscape.Replace(q)+"%")
129		}
130
```
</details></tool-use>
